332ページ。 [[第二次世界大戦]]中の[[1945年]]（[[昭和]]20年）[[7月29日]]、[[宇治山田空襲]]により被災する伊勢市 編（1968）：445ページ。戦後、鎮座地周辺で[[区画整理]]が行われたため、整理前に比べ北側の敷地が縮小し、西側の敷地が拡大した。 [[1980年]]（昭和52年）[[5月10日]]に建て替えられた。 直近では[[2017年]]（[[平成]]29年）[[10月11日]]に建て替えられた。 == 祭祀 == [[祈年祭]]（2月）、[[月次祭]]（6月・12月）、[[神嘗祭]]（10月）、[[新嘗祭]]（11月）は、[[禰宜]]（ねぎ）が巡回祭典の形で境内にて祭祀を執行し、[[歳旦祭]]（1月1日）、[[元始祭]]（1月3日）、[[紀元節|建国記念祭]]（[[2月11日]]）、[[天長祭]]（[[2月23日]]）風日祈祭（5月・8月）は遥祀を行う式内社研究会 編（1990）：332ページ。 == 植物相 == 境内には[[高木]]の[[エノキ]]、[[クスノキ]]のほか中低木の[[アラカシ]]、[[ケヤキ]]が茂る。区画整理で新たに社地となった西側には[[サンゴジュ]]が植えられている。 == 交通 == ; 公共交通 * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[参宮線]] [[山田上口駅]]の東南東約300[[メートル|m]]のところに鎮座する。住宅地の中にあり、北隣に街区公園（旧・児童公園）が、南隣に民家がある。またほど近くに同じく外宮摂社の草奈伎神社・大間国生神社がある伊勢文化舎（2008）：53ページ。 * [[近畿日本鉄道|近鉄]][[近鉄山田線|山田線]] [[宮町駅]]より徒歩で約5分（約450m）。 * [[三重交通]][[路線バス|バス]]「宮町」[[バス停留所|バス停]]より徒歩で約3分（約300m）。 ; 自家用車 * [[伊勢自動車道]] [[伊勢西インターチェンジ|伊勢西IC]]より[[三重県道32号伊勢磯部線]]（御木本道路）を北へ約8分（約3.35[[キロメートル|km]]）。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史』伊勢市役所、昭和43年3月31日、954p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年3月5日、1690p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kiyonoiba-jinja 02.jpg|270px]]
Shrine Hall | Location = 1-8 Tokiwachō, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | Coordinates = {{coord|34|29|42|N|136|41|48.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | Deity = {{ill|Kayanohime|ja|草野姫命}} | Shrine Rank = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor rank), {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Before [[Enryaku era|Enryaku]] 23 (804 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Annual Festival = [[Kinen-sai]] (Prayer Festival), [[Tsukinami-sai]] (Monthly Festival), among others }} '''{{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社}}''' (*Kiyonoiba Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It ranks 9th among the auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): Volume 1, p. 12. == 概要 == [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]常磐一丁目1197番地式内社研究会 編（1990）：329ページ（[[住居表示]]では常磐一丁目8番）に鎮座する。同市宮町一丁目の[[伊勢高柳商店街]]にある今社が旧社地であるとする説がある。この説が正しいとすれば、清野井庭神社が「大間社東野」にあり、草名伎社が「大間社西」にあったとする『類聚神祇本源』の記述に照らして、[[草奈伎神社|草奈伎神社・大間国生神社]]の位置もずれることになる式内社研究会 編（1990）：330ページ。この問題に関して『宇治山田市史』では、往古の大間国生神社は[[江戸時代]]の同社よりも広い社地を持っていたと考えられるが、清野井庭神社の再興時点での社地を基準に考えてしまったため現社地に鎮座したのであり、今社が式内社の清野井庭神社の本来の社地であるとしている宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：下巻936 - 937ページ。社地は[[長方形]]で[[面積]]は1,095[[平方メートル|m2]]である式内社研究会 編（1990）：333ページ。 [[祭神]]は[[カヤノヒメ|草野姫命]]（かやのひめのみこと伊勢文化舎（2008）：52ページ）。鎮座地近隣の[[用水路|灌漑用水]]の[[神 (神道)|神]]であるが、別説では屋船の神の[[分霊]]であるという伊勢文化舎（2008）：52 - 53ページ。『神名帳考證再考』では{{ill|Shitomi Shrine|ja|志等美神社}}と祭神が同じであるとしている。 鳥居は東向きに立っており、[[社殿]]は鳥居から入ってすぐ右手に建つ式内社研究会 編（1990）：334ページ。[[神明造]]の[[板葺]]で、写真にあるように[[玉垣]]に囲まれている。[[賽銭]]箱は置かれていない。 == 歴史 == [[File:Kiyonoibajinja sengu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|[[2017年]]（[[平成]]29年）[[10月11日]]の遷宮当日の様子。]] 社名・清野井庭神社の「清野」は鎮座地周辺の[[原野]]を指し、「井庭」は[[堰|井堰]]を意味する。「井庭」は[[時代]]や[[書物]]により、「いば」・「いにわ」・「いて」と呼んでいた。 伊勢神宮の摂社の定義より『[[延喜式神名帳]]』成立、すなわち[[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）以前に創建された。また、『止由気宮儀式帳』にも記載がある式内社研究会 編（1990）：331ページことから[[延暦]]23年（[[804年]]）以前から存在したことになる。その後[[中世]]の末には[[祭祀]]が行われなくなり、近世には社地不明となるが、[[大宮司]]・[[河辺精長|河邊精長]]の尽力により[[寛文]]3年[[6月21日 (旧暦)|6月21日]]（[[グレゴリオ暦]]：[[1663年]][[7月25日]]）に現社地で再興された式内社研究会 編（1990）：329 - 332ページ。 [[第二次世界大戦]]中の[[1945年]]（[[昭和]]20年）[[7月29日]]、[[宇治山田空襲]]により被災する伊勢市 編（1968）：445ページ。戦後、鎮座地周辺で[[区画整理]]が行われたため、整理前に比べ北側の敷地が縮小し、西側の敷地が拡大した。 [[1980年]]（昭和52年）[[5月10日]]に建て替えられた。 直近では[[2017年]]（[[平成]]29年）[[10月11日]]に建て替えられた。 == 祭祀 == [[祈年祭]]（2月）、[[月次祭]]（6月・12月）、[[神嘗祭]]（10月）、[[新嘗祭]]（11月）は、[[禰宜]]（ねぎ）が巡回祭典の形で境内にて祭祀を執行し、[[歳旦祭]]（1月1日）、[[元始祭]]（1月3日）、[[紀元節|建国記念祭]]（[[2月11日]]）、[[天長祭]]（[[2月23日]]）風日祈祭（5月・8月）は遥祀を行う式内社研究会 編（1990）：332ページ。 == 植物相 == 境内には[[高木]]の[[エノキ]]、[[クスノキ]]のほか中低木の[[アラカシ]]、[[ケヤキ]]が茂る。区画整理で新たに社地となった西側には[[サンゴジュ]]が植えられている。 == 交通 == ; 公共交通 * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[参宮線]] [[山田上口駅]]の東南東約300[[メートル|m]]のところに鎮座する。住宅地の中にあり、北隣に街区公園（旧・児童公園）が、南隣に民家がある。またほど近くに同じく外宮摂社の草奈伎神社・大間国生神社がある伊勢文化舎（2008）：53ページ。 * [[近畿日本鉄道|近鉄]][[近鉄山田線|山田線]] [[宮町駅]]より徒歩で約5分（約450m）。 * [[三重交通]][[路線バス|バス]]「宮町」[[バス停留所|バス停]]より徒歩で約3分（約300m）。 ; 自家用車 * [[伊勢自動車道]] [[伊勢西インターチェンジ|伊勢西IC]]より[[三重県道32号伊勢磯部線]]（御木本道路）を北へ約8分（約3.35[[キロメートル|km]]）。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史』伊勢市役所、昭和43年3月31日、954p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年3月5日、1690p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == 関連項目 == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0601.html 清野井庭神社（きよのいばじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|きよのいはしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]][[ja:清野井庭神社]][[Category:untranslated pages]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:清野井庭神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]]

公共交通 * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[参宮線]] [[山田上口駅]]の東南東約300[[メートル|m]]のところに鎮座する。住宅地の中にあり、北隣に街区公園（旧・児童公園）が、南隣に民家がある。またほど近くに同じく外宮摂社の草奈伎神社・大間国生神社がある伊勢文化舎（2008）：53ページ。 * [[近畿日本鉄道|近鉄]][[近鉄山田線|山田線]] [[宮町駅]]より徒歩で約5分（約450m）。 * [[三重交通]][[路線バス|バス]]「宮町」[[バス停留所|バス停]]より徒歩で約3分（約300m）。 ; 自家用車 * [[伊勢自動車道]] [[伊勢西インターチェンジ|伊勢西IC]]より[[三重県道32号伊勢磯部線]]（御木本道路）を北へ約8分（約3.35[[キロメートル|km]]）。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史』伊勢市役所、昭和43年3月31日、954p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年3月5日、1690p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == 関連項目 == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0601.html 清野井庭神社（きよのいばじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|きよのいはしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]][[ja:清野井庭神社]][[Category:untranslated pages]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:清野井庭神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]]

{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kiyonoiba-jinja 02.jpg|270px]]
Shrine Hall | location = 1-8 Tokiwachō, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | Coordinates = {{coord|34|29|42|N|136|41|48.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Kayanohime|ja|草野姫命}} | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor rank), {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Before [[Enryaku era|Enryaku]] 23 (804 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[Kinen-sai]] (Prayer Festival), [[Tsukinami-sai]] (Monthly Festival), among others }} '''{{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社}}''' (*Kiyonoiba Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It ranks 9th among the auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): Volume 1, p. 12. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}} Tokiwa 1-chome, 1197 Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 329 (in the residential address system: Tokiwa 1-chome, 8) is where the shrine is located. There is a theory that the former shrine site was at the {{ill|Ise Takayanagi Shopping Street|ja|伊勢高柳商店街}} in Miyamachi 1-chome, Ise City . If this theory is correct, it would suggest a different position for {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社}} and {{ill|Oma Kokuso Shrine|ja|大間国生神社}} as described in "Ruiju Jingihongen" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 330. The "Ujiyamada City History" suggests that the original Oma Kokuso Shrine had a larger precinct and that Kiyonoiba Shrine was established on the current site based on the situation during its reconstruction Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 936-937. The shrine grounds are rectangular, with an area of 1,095 square meters Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 333. The {{ill|enshrined deity|ja|祭神}} is {{ill|Kayanohime|ja|カヤノヒメ}} (Kayanohime no Mikoto Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 52). The deity is associated with the {{ill|irrigation water|ja|用水路}} near the shrine, though another theory suggests it is a {{ill|spirit|ja|分霊}} of the deity of shipbuilding Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 52-53. "Jinmyocho Kosho Saiko" states that the enshrined deity is the same as {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|ja|志等美神社}}. The {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} faces east, and the {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}} is located immediately to the right after entering Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 334. Built in {{ill|Shimmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with wooden planks, it is surrounded by a {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} . There is no offering box. == History == [[File:Kiyonoibajinja sengu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Scene from the reconstruction on October 11, 2017.]] The name "Kiyonoiba" refers to the {{ill|wilderness|ja|原野}} around the shrine , while "Iba" means {{ill|weir|ja|堰}} . Over different {{ill|eras|ja|時代}} and {{ill|texts|ja|書物}}, "Iba" has been read as "iba," "iniwa," or "ite" . The shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, meaning it predates 927. It also appears in the "Toyukemiya Gishikicho" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 331, indicating its existence before 804. Rituals ceased by the late {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世}}, and the site was forgotten until {{ill|Oomiya-ji|ja|大宮司}} {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長}} restored it on June 21, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: July 25, 1663) Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 329-332. During {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, the shrine was damaged in the {{ill|Ujiyamada Air Raid|ja|宇治山田空襲}} on July 29, 1945 Ise City (1968): p. 445. Postwar {{ill|land readjustment|ja|区画整理}} reduced the northern area and expanded the western part . The shrine was rebuilt on May 10, 1980 and again on October 11, 2017. == Rituals == {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November) are conducted by a {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}} at the shrine. {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}} (January 3), {{ill|National Foundation Day|ja|建国記念祭}} (February 11), {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭}} (February 23), and {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja|風日祈祭}} (May, August) are held remotely Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 332. == Flora == The precinct has tall trees such as {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ}} and {{ill|camphor trees|ja|クスノキ}}, along with mid-sized trees like {{ill|arakashi|ja|アラカシ}} and {{ill|zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} . Newly added western areas have {{ill|coral trees|ja|サンゴジュ}} . == Access == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}}: About 300m southeast of {{ill|Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|ja|山田上口駅}} . * {{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道}} {{ill|Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}: Around 5 minutes' walk (450m) from {{ill|Miyamachi Station|ja|宮町駅}}. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} {{ill|Bus|ja|路線バス}}: 3-minute walk (300m) from "Miyamachi" {{ill|bus stop|ja|バス停留所}}. ; By Car * {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}: About 8 minutes (3.35 km) north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mikimoto Road). [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:清野井庭神社]] == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史』伊勢市役所、昭和43年3月31日、954p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年3月5日、1690p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == 関連項目 == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0601.html 清野井庭神社（きよのいばじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|きよのいはしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]][[ja:清野井庭神社]][[Category:untranslated pages]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:清野井庭神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kiyonoiba-jinja 02.jpg|270px]]
Shrine Hall | location = 1-8 Tokiwachō, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | Coordinates = {{coord|34|29|42|N|136|41|48.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Kayanohime|ja|草野姫命}} | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor rank), {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Before [[Enryaku era|Enryaku]] 23 (804 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[Kinen-sai]] (Prayer Festival), [[Tsukinami-sai]] (Monthly Festival), among others }} '''{{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社}}''' (*Kiyonoiba Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It ranks 9th among the auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): Volume 1, p. 12. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}} Tokiwa 1-chome, 1197 Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 329 (in the residential address system: Tokiwa 1-chome, 8) is where the shrine is located. There is a theory that the former shrine site was at the {{ill|Ise Takayanagi Shopping Street|ja|伊勢高柳商店街}} in Miyamachi 1-chome, Ise City . If this theory is correct, it would suggest a different position for {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社}} and {{ill|Oma Kokuso Shrine|ja|大間国生神社}} as described in "Ruiju Jingihongen" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 330. The "Ujiyamada City History" suggests that the original Oma Kokuso Shrine had a larger precinct and that Kiyonoiba Shrine was established on the current site based on the situation during its reconstruction Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 936-937. The shrine grounds are rectangular, with an area of 1,095 square meters Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 333. The {{ill|enshrined deity|ja|祭神}} is {{ill|Kayanohime|ja|カヤノヒメ}} (Kayanohime no Mikoto Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 52). The deity is associated with the {{ill|irrigation water|ja|用水路}} near the shrine, though another theory suggests it is a {{ill|spirit|ja|分霊}} of the deity of shipbuilding Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 52-53. "Jinmyocho Kosho Saiko" states that the enshrined deity is the same as {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|ja|志等美神社}}. The {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} faces east, and the {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}} is located immediately to the right after entering Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 334. Built in {{ill|Shimmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with wooden planks, it is surrounded by a {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} . There is no offering box. == History == [[File:Kiyonoibajinja sengu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Scene from the reconstruction on October 11, 2017.]] The name "Kiyonoiba" refers to the {{ill|wilderness|ja|原野}} around the shrine , while "Iba" means {{ill|weir|ja|堰}} . Over different {{ill|eras|ja|時代}} and {{ill|texts|ja|書物}}, "Iba" has been read as "iba," "iniwa," or "ite" . The shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, meaning it predates 927. It also appears in the "Toyukemiya Gishikicho" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 331, indicating its existence before 804. Rituals ceased by the late {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世}}, and the site was forgotten until {{ill|Oomiya-ji|ja|大宮司}} {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長}} restored it on June 21, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: July 25, 1663) Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 329-332. During {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, the shrine was damaged in the {{ill|Ujiyamada Air Raid|ja|宇治山田空襲}} on July 29, 1945 Ise City (1968): p. 445. Postwar {{ill|land readjustment|ja|区画整理}} reduced the northern area and expanded the western part . The shrine was rebuilt on May 10, 1980 and again on October 11, 2017. == Rituals == {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November) are conducted by a {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}} at the shrine. {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}} (January 3), {{ill|National Foundation Day|ja|建国記念祭}} (February 11), {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭}} (February 23), and {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja|風日祈祭}} (May, August) are held remotely Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 332. == Flora == The precinct has tall trees such as {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ}} and {{ill|camphor trees|ja|クスノキ}}, along with mid-sized trees like {{ill|arakashi|ja|アラカシ}} and {{ill|zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} . Newly added western areas have {{ill|coral trees|ja|サンゴジュ}} . == Access == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}}: About 300m southeast of {{ill|Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|ja|山田上口駅}} . * {{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道}} {{ill|Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}: Around 5 minutes' walk (450m) from {{ill|Miyamachi Station|ja|宮町駅}}. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} {{ill|Bus|ja|路線バス}}: 3-minute walk (300m) from "Miyamachi" {{ill|bus stop|ja|バス停留所}}. ; By Car * {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}: About 8 minutes (3.35 km) north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mikimoto Road). [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:清野井庭神社]] == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史』伊勢市役所、昭和43年3月31日、954p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年3月5日、1690p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == 関連項目 == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0601.html 清野井庭神社（きよのいばじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|きよのいはしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]][[ja:清野井庭神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:清野井庭神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]]

Rituals ceased by the late {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世}}, and the site was forgotten until {{ill|Oomiya-ji|ja|大宮司}} {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長}} restored it on June 21, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: July 25, 1663) Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 329-332. During {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, the shrine was damaged in the {{ill|Ujiyamada Air Raid|ja|宇治山田空襲}} on July 29, 1945 Ise City (1968): p. 445. Postwar {{ill|land readjustment|ja|区画整理}} reduced the northern area and expanded the western part . The shrine was rebuilt on May 10, 1980 and again on October 11, 2017. == Rituals == {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November) are conducted by a {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}} at the shrine. {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}} (January 3), {{ill|National Foundation Day|ja|建国記念祭}} (February 11), {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭}} (February 23), and {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja|風日祈祭}} (May, August) are held remotely Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 332. == Flora == The precinct has tall trees such as {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ}} and {{ill|camphor trees|ja|クスノキ}}, along with mid-sized trees like {{ill|arakashi|ja|アラカシ}} and {{ill|zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} . Newly added western areas have {{ill|coral trees|ja|サンゴジュ}} . == Access == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}}: About 300m southeast of {{ill|Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|ja|山田上口駅}} . * {{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道}} {{ill|Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}: Around 5 minutes' walk (450m) from {{ill|Miyamachi Station|ja|宮町駅}}. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} {{ill|Bus|ja|路線バス}}: 3-minute walk (300m) from "Miyamachi" {{ill|bus stop|ja|バス停留所}}. ; By Car * {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}: About 8 minutes (3.35 km) north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mikimoto Road). [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:清野井庭神社]] == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史』伊勢市役所、昭和43年3月31日、954p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年3月5日、1690p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == 関連項目 == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0601.html 清野井庭神社（きよのいばじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|きよのいはしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Kiyonoiba Shrine-qq]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]][[ja:清野井庭神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:清野井庭神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kiyonoiba-jinja 02.jpg|270px]]
Shrine Hall | location = 1-8 Tokiwachō, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | Coordinates = {{coord|34|29|42|N|136|41|48.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Kayanohime|ja|草野姫命}} | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor rank), {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Before [[Enryaku era|Enryaku]] 23 (804 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[Kinen-sai]] (Prayer Festival), [[Tsukinami-sai]] (Monthly Festival), among others }} '''{{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社}}''' (*Kiyonoiba Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It ranks 9th among the auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): Volume 1, p. 12. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}} Tokiwa 1-chome, 1197 Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 329 (in the residential address system: Tokiwa 1-chome, 8) is where the shrine is located. There is a theory that the former shrine site was at the {{ill|Ise Takayanagi Shopping Street|ja|伊勢高柳商店街}} in Miyamachi 1-chome, Ise City . If this theory is correct, it would suggest a different position for {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社}} and {{ill|Oma Kokuso Shrine|ja|大間国生神社}} as described in "Ruiju Jingihongen" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 330. The "Ujiyamada City History" suggests that the original Oma Kokuso Shrine had a larger precinct and that Kiyonoiba Shrine was established on the current site based on the situation during its reconstruction Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 936-937. The shrine grounds are rectangular, with an area of 1,095 square meters Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 333. The {{ill|enshrined deity|ja|deity}} is {{ill|Kayanohime|ja|カヤノヒメ}} (Kayanohime no Mikoto Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 52). The deity is associated with the {{ill|irrigation water|ja|用水路}} near the shrine, though another theory suggests it is a {{ill|spirit|ja|分霊}} of the deity of shipbuilding Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 52-53. "Jinmyocho Kosho Saiko" states that the enshrined deity is the same as {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|ja|志等美神社}}. The {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} faces east, and the {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}} is located immediately to the right after entering Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 334. Built in {{ill|Shimmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with wooden planks, it is surrounded by a {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} . There is no offering box. == History == [[File:Kiyonoibajinja sengu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Scene from the reconstruction on October 11, 2017.]] The name "Kiyonoiba" refers to the {{ill|wilderness|ja|原野}} around the shrine , while "Iba" means {{ill|weir|ja|堰}} . Over different {{ill|eras|ja|時代}} and {{ill|texts|ja|書物}}, "Iba" has been read as "iba," "iniwa," or "ite" . The shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, meaning it predates 927. It also appears in the "Toyukemiya Gishikicho" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 331, indicating its existence before 804. Rituals ceased by the late {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世}}, and the site was forgotten until {{ill|Oomiya-ji|ja|大宮司}} {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長}} restored it on June 21, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: July 25, 1663) Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 329-332. During {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, the shrine was damaged in the {{ill|Ujiyamada Air Raid|ja|宇治山田空襲}} on July 29, 1945 Ise City (1968): p. 445. Postwar {{ill|land readjustment|ja|区画整理}} reduced the northern area and expanded the western part . The shrine was rebuilt on May 10, 1980 and again on October 11, 2017. == Rituals == {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November) are conducted by a {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}} at the shrine. {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}} (January 3), {{ill|National Foundation Day|ja|建国記念祭}} (February 11), {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭}} (February 23), and {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja|風日祈祭}} (May, August) are held remotely Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 332. == Flora == The precinct has tall trees such as {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ}} and {{ill|camphor trees|ja|クスノキ}}, along with mid-sized trees like {{ill|arakashi|ja|アラカシ}} and {{ill|zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} . Newly added western areas have {{ill|coral trees|ja|サンゴジュ}} . == Access == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}}: About 300m southeast of {{ill|Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|ja|山田上口駅}} . * {{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道}} {{ill|Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}: Around 5 minutes' walk (450m) from {{ill|Miyamachi Station|ja|宮町駅}}. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} {{ill|Bus|ja|路線バス}}: 3-minute walk (300m) from "Miyamachi" {{ill|bus stop|ja|バス停留所}}. ; By Car * {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}: About 8 minutes (3.35 km) north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mikimoto Road). [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:清野井庭神社]] == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史』伊勢市役所、昭和43年3月31日、954p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年3月5日、1690p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == 関連項目 == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0601.html 清野井庭神社（きよのいばじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|きよのいはしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Kiyonoiba Shrine-qq]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]][[ja:清野井庭神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:清野井庭神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]]

The deity is associated with the {{ill|irrigation water|ja|用水路}} near the shrine, though another theory suggests it is a {{ill|spirit|ja|分霊}} of the deity of shipbuilding Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 52-53. "Jinmyocho Kosho Saiko" states that the enshrined deity is the same as {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|ja|志等美神社}}. The {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} faces east, and the {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}} is located immediately to the right after entering Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 334. Built in {{ill|Shimmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with wooden planks, it is surrounded by a {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} . There is no offering box. == History == [[File:Kiyonoibajinja sengu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Scene from the reconstruction on October 11, 2017.]] The name "Kiyonoiba" refers to the {{ill|wilderness|ja|原野}} around the shrine , while "Iba" means {{ill|weir|ja|堰}} . Over different {{ill|eras|ja|時代}} and {{ill|texts|ja|書物}}, "Iba" has been read as "iba," "iniwa," or "ite" . The shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, meaning it predates 927. It also appears in the "Toyukemiya Gishikicho" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 331, indicating its existence before 804. Rituals ceased by the late {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世}}, and the site was forgotten until {{ill|Oomiya-ji|ja|大宮司}} {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長}} restored it on June 21, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: July 25, 1663) Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 329-332. During {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, the shrine was damaged in the {{ill|Ujiyamada Air Raid|ja|宇治山田空襲}} on July 29, 1945 Ise City (1968): p. 445. Postwar {{ill|land readjustment|ja|区画整理}} reduced the northern area and expanded the western part . The shrine was rebuilt on May 10, 1980 and again on October 11, 2017. == Rituals == {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November) are conducted by a {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}} at the shrine. {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}} (January 3), {{ill|National Foundation Day|ja|建国記念祭}} (February 11), {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭}} (February 23), and {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja|風日祈祭}} (May, August) are held remotely Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 332. == Flora == The precinct has tall trees such as {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ}} and {{ill|camphor trees|ja|クスノキ}}, along with mid-sized trees like {{ill|arakashi|ja|アラカシ}} and {{ill|zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} . Newly added western areas have {{ill|coral trees|ja|サンゴジュ}} . == Access == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}}: About 300m southeast of {{ill|Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|ja|山田上口駅}} . * {{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道}} {{ill|Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}: Around 5 minutes' walk (450m) from {{ill|Miyamachi Station|ja|宮町駅}}. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} {{ill|Bus|ja|路線バス}}: 3-minute walk (300m) from "Miyamachi" {{ill|bus stop|ja|バス停留所}}. ; By Car * {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}: About 8 minutes (3.35 km) north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mikimoto Road). [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:清野井庭神社]] == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史』伊勢市役所、昭和43年3月31日、954p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年3月5日、1690p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == 関連項目 == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0601.html 清野井庭神社（きよのいばじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|きよのいはしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kiyonoiba Shrine-qq]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]][[ja:清野井庭神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:清野井庭神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]]

{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kiyonoiba-jinja 02.jpg|270px]]
Shrine Hall | location = 1-8 Tokiwachō, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | Coordinates = {{coord|34|29|42|N|136|41|48.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Kayanohime|ja|草野姫命}} | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor rank), {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Before [[Enryaku era|Enryaku]] 23 (804 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[Kinen-sai]] (Prayer Festival), [[Tsukinami-sai]] (Monthly Festival), among others }} '''{{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社}}''' (*Kiyonoiba Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It ranks 9th among the auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): Volume 1, p. 12. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}} Tokiwa 1-chome, 1197 Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 329 (in the residential address system: Tokiwa 1-chome, 8) is where the shrine is located. There is a theory that the former shrine site was at the {{ill|Ise Takayanagi Shopping Street|ja|伊勢高柳商店街}} in Miyamachi 1-chome, Ise City . If this theory is correct, it would suggest a different position for {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社}} and {{ill|Oma Kokuso Shrine|ja|大間国生神社}} as described in "Ruiju Jingihongen" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 330. The "Ujiyamada City History" suggests that the original Oma Kokuso Shrine had a larger precinct and that Kiyonoiba Shrine was established on the current site based on the situation during its reconstruction Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 936-937. The shrine grounds are rectangular, with an area of 1,095 square meters Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 333. The {{ill|enshrined deity|ja|deity}} is {{ill|Kayanohime|ja|カヤノヒメ}} (Kayanohime no Mikoto Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 52). The deity is associated with the {{ill|irrigation water|ja|用水路}} near the shrine, though another theory suggests it is a {{ill|spirit|ja|分霊}} of the deity of shipbuilding Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 52-53. "Jinmyocho Kosho Saiko" states that the enshrined deity is the same as {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|ja|志等美神社}}. The {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} faces east, and the {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}} is located immediately to the right after entering Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 334. Built in {{ill|Shimmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with wooden planks, it is surrounded by a {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} . There is no offering box. == History == [[File:Kiyonoibajinja sengu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Scene from the reconstruction on October 11, 2017.]] The name "Kiyonoiba" refers to the {{ill|wilderness|ja|原野}} around the shrine , while "Iba" means {{ill|weir|ja|堰}} . The shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, meaning it predates 927. It also appears in the "Toyukemiya Gishikicho" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 331, indicating its existence before 804. Rituals ceased by the late {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世}}, and the site was forgotten until {{ill|Oomiya-ji|ja|大宮司}} {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長}} restored it on June 21, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: July 25, 1663) Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 329-332. During {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, the shrine was damaged in the {{ill|Ujiyamada Air Raid|ja|宇治山田空襲}} on July 29, 1945 Ise City (1968): p. 445. Postwar {{ill|land readjustment|ja|区画整理}} reduced the northern area and expanded the western part . The shrine was rebuilt on May 10, 1980 and again on October 11, 2017. == Rituals == {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November) are conducted by a {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}} at the shrine. {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}} (January 3), {{ill|National Foundation Day|ja|建国記念祭}} (February 11), {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭}} (February 23), and {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja|風日祈祭}} (May, August) are held remotely Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 332. == Flora == The precinct has tall trees such as {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ}} and {{ill|camphor trees|ja|クスノキ}}, along with mid-sized trees like {{ill|arakashi|ja|アラカシ}} and {{ill|zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} . Newly added western areas have {{ill|coral trees|ja|サンゴジュ}} . == Access == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}}: About 300m southeast of {{ill|Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|ja|山田上口駅}} . * {{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道}} {{ill|Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}: Around 5 minutes' walk (450m) from {{ill|Miyamachi Station|ja|宮町駅}}. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} {{ill|Bus|ja|路線バス}}: 3-minute walk (300m) from "Miyamachi" {{ill|bus stop|ja|バス停留所}}. ; By Car * {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}: About 8 minutes (3.35 km) north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mikimoto Road). It ranks 9th among the auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): Volume 1, p. 12. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}} Tokiwa 1-chome, 1197 Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 329 (in the residential address system: Tokiwa 1-chome, 8) is where the shrine is located. There is a theory that the former shrine site was at the {{ill|Ise Takayanagi Shopping Street|ja|伊勢高柳商店街}} in Miyamachi 1-chome, Ise City . If this theory is correct, it would suggest a different position for {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社}} and {{ill|Oma Kokuso Shrine|ja|大間国生神社}} as described in "Ruiju Jingihongen" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 330. The "Ujiyamada City History" suggests that the original Oma Kokuso Shrine had a larger precinct and that Kiyonoiba Shrine was established on the current site based on the situation during its reconstruction Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 936-937. The shrine grounds are rectangular, with an area of 1,095 square meters Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 333. The {{ill|enshrined deity|ja|deity}} is {{ill|Kayanohime|ja|カヤノヒメ}} (Kayanohime no Mikoto Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 52). The deity is associated with the {{ill|irrigation water|ja|用水路}} near the shrine, though another theory suggests it is a {{ill|spirit|ja|分霊}} of the deity of shipbuilding Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 52-53. "Jinmyocho Kosho Saiko" states that the enshrined deity is the same as {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|ja|志等美神社}}. The {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} faces east, and the {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}} is located immediately to the right after entering Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 334. Built in {{ill|Shimmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with wooden planks, it is surrounded by a {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} . There is no offering box. == History == [[File:Kiyonoibajinja sengu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Scene from the reconstruction on October 11, 2017.]] The name "Kiyonoiba" refers to the {{ill|wilderness|ja|原野}} around the shrine , while "Iba" means {{ill|weir|ja|堰}} . Over different {{ill|eras|ja|時代}} and {{ill|texts|ja|書物}}, "Iba" has been read as "iba," "iniwa," or "ite" . The shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, meaning it predates 927. It also appears in the "Toyukemiya Gishikicho" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 331, indicating its existence before 804. Rituals ceased by the late {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世}}, and the site was forgotten until {{ill|Oomiya-ji|ja|大宮司}} {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長}} restored it on June 21, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: July 25, 1663) Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 329-332. During {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, the shrine was damaged in the {{ill|Ujiyamada Air Raid|ja|宇治山田空襲}} on July 29, 1945 Ise City (1968): p. 445. Postwar {{ill|land readjustment|ja|区画整理}} reduced the northern area and expanded the western part . The shrine was rebuilt on May 10, 1980 and again on October 11, 2017. == Rituals == {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November) are conducted by a {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}} at the shrine. {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}} (January 3), {{ill|National Foundation Day|ja|建国記念祭}} (February 11), {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭}} (February 23), and {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja|風日祈祭}} (May, August) are held remotely Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 332. == Flora == The precinct has tall trees such as {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ}} and {{ill|camphor trees|ja|クスノキ}}, along with mid-sized trees like {{ill|arakashi|ja|アラカシ}} and {{ill|zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} . Newly added western areas have {{ill|coral trees|ja|サンゴジュ}} . == Access == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}}: About 300m southeast of {{ill|Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|ja|山田上口駅}} . * {{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道}} {{ill|Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}: Around 5 minutes' walk (450m) from {{ill|Miyamachi Station|ja|宮町駅}}. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} {{ill|Bus|ja|路線バス}}: 3-minute walk (300m) from "Miyamachi" {{ill|bus stop|ja|バス停留所}}. ; By Car * {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}: About 8 minutes (3.35 km) north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mikimoto Road). [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:清野井庭神社]] == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史』伊勢市役所、昭和43年3月31日、954p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年3月5日、1690p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == 関連項目 == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0601.html 清野井庭神社（きよのいばじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|きよのいはしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kiyonoiba Shrine-qq]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]][[ja:清野井庭神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:清野井庭神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:new pages]]

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:Removing this excess]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kiyonoiba-jinja 02.jpg|270px]]
Shrine Hall | location = 1-8 Tokiwachō, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|42|N|136|41|48.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Kayanohime|ja|草野姫命}} | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor rank), {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Before [[Enryaku era|Enryaku]] 23 (804 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[Kinen-sai]] (Prayer Festival), [[Tsukinami-sai]] (Monthly Festival), among others }} '''{{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社}}''' (*Kiyonoiba Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It ranks 9th among the auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): Volume 1, p. 12. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}} Tokiwa 1-chome, 1197 Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 329 (in the residential address system: Tokiwa 1-chome, 8) is where the shrine is located. There is a theory that the former shrine site was at the {{ill|Ise Takayanagi Shopping Street|ja|伊勢高柳商店街}} in Miyamachi 1-chome, Ise City . If this theory is correct, it would suggest a different position for {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社}} and {{ill|Oma Kokuso Shrine|ja|大間国生神社}} as described in "Ruiju Jingihongen" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 330. The "Ujiyamada City History" suggests that the original Oma Kokuso Shrine had a larger precinct and that Kiyonoiba Shrine was established on the current site based on the situation during its reconstruction Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 936-937. The shrine grounds are rectangular, with an area of 1,095 square meters Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 333. The {{ill|enshrined deity|ja|deity}} is {{ill|Kayanohime|ja|カヤノヒメ}} (Kayanohime no Mikoto Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 52). The deity is associated with the {{ill|irrigation water|ja|用水路}} near the shrine, though another theory suggests it is a {{ill|spirit|ja|分霊}} of the deity of shipbuilding Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 52-53. "Jinmyocho Kosho Saiko" states that the enshrined deity is the same as {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|ja|志等美神社}}. The {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} faces east, and the {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}} is located immediately to the right after entering Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 334. Built in {{ill|Shimmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with wooden planks, it is surrounded by a {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} . There is no offering box. == History == [[File:Kiyonoibajinja sengu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Scene from the reconstruction on October 11, 2017.]] The name "Kiyonoiba" refers to the {{ill|wilderness|ja|原野}} around the shrine , while "Iba" means {{ill|weir|ja|堰}} . Over different {{ill|eras|ja|時代}} and {{ill|texts|ja|書物}}, "Iba" has been read as "iba," "iniwa," or "ite" . The shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, meaning it predates 927. It also appears in the "Toyukemiya Gishikicho" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 331, indicating its existence before 804. Rituals ceased by the late {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世}}, and the site was forgotten until {{ill|Oomiya-ji|ja|大宮司}} {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長}} restored it on June 21, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: July 25, 1663) Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 329-332. During {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, the shrine was damaged in the {{ill|Ujiyamada Air Raid|ja|宇治山田空襲}} on July 29, 1945 Ise City (1968): p. 445. Postwar {{ill|land readjustment|ja|区画整理}} reduced the northern area and expanded the western part . The shrine was rebuilt on May 10, 1980 and again on October 11, 2017. == Rituals == {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November) are conducted by a {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}} at the shrine. {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}} (January 3), {{ill|National Foundation Day|ja|建国記念祭}} (February 11), {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭}} (February 23), and {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja|風日祈祭}} (May, August) are held remotely Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 332. == Flora == The precinct has tall trees such as {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ}} and {{ill|camphor trees|ja|クスノキ}}, along with mid-sized trees like {{ill|arakashi|ja|アラカシ}} and {{ill|zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} . Newly added western areas have {{ill|coral trees|ja|サンゴジュ}} . == Access == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}}: About 300m southeast of {{ill|Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|ja|山田上口駅}} . * {{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道}} {{ill|Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}: Around 5 minutes' walk (450m) from {{ill|Miyamachi Station|ja|宮町駅}}. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} {{ill|Bus|ja|路線バス}}: 3-minute walk (300m) from "Miyamachi" {{ill|bus stop|ja|バス停留所}}. ; By Car * {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}: About 8 minutes (3.35 km) north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mikimoto Road). [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:清野井庭神社]] == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史』伊勢市役所、昭和43年3月31日、954p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年3月5日、1690p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == 関連項目 == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0601.html 清野井庭神社（きよのいばじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|きよのいはしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kiyonoiba Shrine-qq]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]][[ja:清野井庭神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:清野井庭神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Removing this excess]]

ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == 関連項目 == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0601.html 清野井庭神社（きよのいばじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|きよのいはしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kiyonoiba Shrine-qq]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]][[ja:清野井庭神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:清野井庭神社]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "豊受大神宮}}" to "豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}}"
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kiyonoiba-jinja 02.jpg|270px]]
Shrine Hall | location = 1-8 Tokiwachō, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|42|N|136|41|48.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Kayanohime|ja|草野姫命}} | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor rank), {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Before [[Enryaku era|Enryaku]] 23 (804 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[Kinen-sai]] (Prayer Festival), [[Tsukinami-sai]] (Monthly Festival), among others }} '''{{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社}}''' (*Kiyonoiba Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It ranks 9th among the auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): Volume 1, p. 12. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}} Tokiwa 1-chome, 1197 Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 329 (in the residential address system: Tokiwa 1-chome, 8) is where the shrine is located. There is a theory that the former shrine site was at the {{ill|Ise Takayanagi Shopping Street|ja|伊勢高柳商店街}} in Miyamachi 1-chome, Ise City . If this theory is correct, it would suggest a different position for {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社}} and {{ill|Oma Kokuso Shrine|ja|大間国生神社}} as described in "Ruiju Jingihongen" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 330. The "Ujiyamada City History" suggests that the original Oma Kokuso Shrine had a larger precinct and that Kiyonoiba Shrine was established on the current site based on the situation during its reconstruction Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 936-937. The shrine grounds are rectangular, with an area of 1,095 square meters Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 333. The {{ill|enshrined deity|ja|deity}} is {{ill|Kayanohime|ja|カヤノヒメ}} (Kayanohime no Mikoto Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 52). The deity is associated with the {{ill|irrigation water|ja|用水路}} near the shrine, though another theory suggests it is a {{ill|spirit|ja|分霊}} of the deity of shipbuilding Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 52-53. "Jinmyocho Kosho Saiko" states that the enshrined deity is the same as {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|ja|志等美神社}}. The {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} faces east, and the {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}} is located immediately to the right after entering Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 334. Built in {{ill|Shimmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with wooden planks, it is surrounded by a {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} . There is no offering box. == History == [[File:Kiyonoibajinja sengu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Scene from the reconstruction on October 11, 2017.]] The name "Kiyonoiba" refers to the {{ill|wilderness|ja|原野}} around the shrine , while "Iba" means {{ill|weir|ja|堰}} . Over different {{ill|eras|ja|時代}} and {{ill|texts|ja|書物}}, "Iba" has been read as "iba," "iniwa," or "ite" . The shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, meaning it predates 927. It also appears in the "Toyukemiya Gishikicho" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 331, indicating its existence before 804. Rituals ceased by the late {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世}}, and the site was forgotten until {{ill|Oomiya-ji|ja|大宮司}} {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長}} restored it on June 21, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: July 25, 1663) Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 329-332. During {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, the shrine was damaged in the {{ill|Ujiyamada Air Raid|ja|宇治山田空襲}} on July 29, 1945 Ise City (1968): p. 445. Postwar {{ill|land readjustment|ja|区画整理}} reduced the northern area and expanded the western part . The shrine was rebuilt on May 10, 1980 and again on October 11, 2017. == Rituals == {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November) are conducted by a {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}} at the shrine. {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}} (January 3), {{ill|National Foundation Day|ja|建国記念祭}} (February 11), {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭}} (February 23), and {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja|風日祈祭}} (May, August) are held remotely Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 332. == Flora == The precinct has tall trees such as {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ}} and {{ill|camphor trees|ja|クスノキ}}, along with mid-sized trees like {{ill|arakashi|ja|アラカシ}} and {{ill|zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} . Newly added western areas have {{ill|coral trees|ja|サンゴジュ}} . == Access == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}}: About 300m southeast of {{ill|Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|ja|山田上口駅}} . * {{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道}} {{ill|Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}: Around 5 minutes' walk (450m) from {{ill|Miyamachi Station|ja|宮町駅}}. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} {{ill|Bus|ja|路線バス}}: 3-minute walk (300m) from "Miyamachi" {{ill|bus stop|ja|バス停留所}}. ; By Car * {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}: About 8 minutes (3.35 km) north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mikimoto Road). [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:清野井庭神社]] == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史』伊勢市役所、昭和43年3月31日、954p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年3月5日、1690p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == 関連項目 == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0601.html 清野井庭神社（きよのいばじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|きよのいはしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kiyonoiba Shrine-qq]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]][[ja:清野井庭神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:清野井庭神社]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

Postwar {{ill|land readjustment|ja|区画整理}} reduced the northern area and expanded the western part . The shrine was rebuilt on May 10, 1980 and again on October 11, 2017. == Rituals == {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November) are conducted by a {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}} at the shrine. {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}} (January 3), {{ill|National Foundation Day|ja|建国記念祭}} (February 11), {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭}} (February 23), and {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja|風日祈祭}} (May, August) are held remotely Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 332. == Flora == The precinct has tall trees such as {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ}} and {{ill|camphor trees|ja|クスノキ}}, along with mid-sized trees like {{ill|arakashi|ja|アラカシ}} and {{ill|zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} . Newly added western areas have {{ill|coral trees|ja|サンゴジュ}} . == Access == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}}: About 300m southeast of {{ill|Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|ja|山田上口駅}} . * {{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道}} {{ill|Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}: Around 5 minutes' walk (450m) from {{ill|Miyamachi Station|ja|宮町駅}}. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} {{ill|Bus|ja|路線バス}}: 3-minute walk (300m) from "Miyamachi" {{ill|bus stop|ja|バス停留所}}. ; By Car * {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}: About 8 minutes (3.35 km) north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mikimoto Road). [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:清野井庭神社]] == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史』伊勢市役所、昭和43年3月31日、954p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年3月5日、1690p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0601.html 清野井庭神社（きよのいばじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|きよのいはしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kiyonoiba Shrine-qq]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]][[ja:清野井庭神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:清野井庭神社]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kiyonoiba-jinja 02.jpg|270px]]
Shrine Hall | location = 1-8 Tokiwachō, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|42|N|136|41|48.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Kayanohime|ja|草野姫命}} | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor rank), {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Before [[Enryaku era|Enryaku]] 23 (804 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[Kinen-sai]] (Prayer Festival), [[Tsukinami-sai]] (Monthly Festival), among others }} '''{{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社}}''' (*Kiyonoiba Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It ranks 9th among the auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): Volume 1, p. 12. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}} Tokiwa 1-chome, 1197 Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 329 (in the residential address system: Tokiwa 1-chome, 8) is where the shrine is located. There is a theory that the former shrine site was at the {{ill|Ise Takayanagi Shopping Street|ja|伊勢高柳商店街}} in Miyamachi 1-chome, Ise City . If this theory is correct, it would suggest a different position for {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社}} and {{ill|Oma Kokuso Shrine|ja|大間国生神社}} as described in "Ruiju Jingihongen" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 330. The "Ujiyamada City History" suggests that the original Oma Kokuso Shrine had a larger precinct and that Kiyonoiba Shrine was established on the current site based on the situation during its reconstruction Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 936-937. The shrine grounds are rectangular, with an area of 1,095 square meters Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 333. The {{ill|enshrined deity|ja|deity}} is {{ill|Kayanohime|ja|カヤノヒメ}} (Kayanohime no Mikoto Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 52). The deity is associated with the {{ill|irrigation water|ja|用水路}} near the shrine, though another theory suggests it is a {{ill|spirit|ja|分霊}} of the deity of shipbuilding Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 52-53. "Jinmyocho Kosho Saiko" states that the enshrined deity is the same as {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|ja|志等美神社}}. The {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} faces east, and the {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}} is located immediately to the right after entering Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 334. Built in {{ill|Shimmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with wooden planks, it is surrounded by a {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} . There is no offering box. == History == [[File:Kiyonoibajinja sengu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Scene from the reconstruction on October 11, 2017.]] The name "Kiyonoiba" refers to the {{ill|wilderness|ja|原野}} around the shrine , while "Iba" means {{ill|weir|ja|堰}} . Over different {{ill|eras|ja|時代}} and {{ill|texts|ja|書物}}, "Iba" has been read as "iba," "iniwa," or "ite" . The shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, meaning it predates 927. It also appears in the "Toyukemiya Gishikicho" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 331, indicating its existence before 804. Rituals ceased by the late {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世}}, and the site was forgotten until {{ill|Oomiya-ji|ja|大宮司}} {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長}} restored it on June 21, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: July 25, 1663) Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 329-332. During {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, the shrine was damaged in the {{ill|Ujiyamada Air Raid|ja|宇治山田空襲}} on July 29, 1945 Ise City (1968): p. 445. Postwar {{ill|land readjustment|ja|区画整理}} reduced the northern area and expanded the western part . The shrine was rebuilt on May 10, 1980 and again on October 11, 2017. == Rituals == {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November) are conducted by a {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}} at the shrine. {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}} (January 3), {{ill|National Foundation Day|ja|建国記念祭}} (February 11), {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭}} (February 23), and {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja|風日祈祭}} (May, August) are held remotely Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 332. == Flora == The precinct has tall trees such as {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ}} and {{ill|camphor trees|ja|クスノキ}}, along with mid-sized trees like {{ill|arakashi|ja|アラカシ}} and {{ill|zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} . Newly added western areas have {{ill|coral trees|ja|サンゴジュ}} . == Access == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}}: About 300m southeast of {{ill|Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|ja|山田上口駅}} . * {{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道}} {{ill|Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}: Around 5 minutes' walk (450m) from {{ill|Miyamachi Station|ja|宮町駅}}. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} {{ill|Bus|ja|路線バス}}: 3-minute walk (300m) from "Miyamachi" {{ill|bus stop|ja|バス停留所}}. ; By Car * {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}: About 8 minutes (3.35 km) north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mikimoto Road). [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:清野井庭神社]] == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史』伊勢市役所、昭和43年3月31日、954p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年3月5日、1690p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0601.html 清野井庭神社（きよのいばじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|きよのいはしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]][[Category:Ugoodbye $1]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kiyonoiba Shrine-qq]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]][[ja:清野井庭神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:清野井庭神社]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

It ranks 9th among the auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): Volume 1, p. 12. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}} Tokiwa 1-chome, 1197 Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 329 (in the residential address system: Tokiwa 1-chome, 8) is where the shrine is located. There is a theory that the former shrine site was at the {{ill|Ise Takayanagi Shopping Street|ja|伊勢高柳商店街}} in Miyamachi 1-chome, Ise City . If this theory is correct, it would suggest a different position for {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社}} and {{ill|Oma Kokuso Shrine|ja|大間国生神社}} as described in "Ruiju Jingihongen" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 330. The "Ujiyamada City History" suggests that the original Oma Kokuso Shrine had a larger precinct and that Kiyonoiba Shrine was established on the current site based on the situation during its reconstruction Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 936-937. The shrine grounds are rectangular, with an area of 1,095 square meters Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 333. The {{ill|enshrined deity|ja|deity}} is {{ill|Kayanohime|ja|カヤノヒメ}} (Kayanohime no Mikoto Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 52). The deity is associated with the {{ill|irrigation water|ja|用水路}} near the shrine, though another theory suggests it is a {{ill|spirit|ja|分霊}} of the deity of shipbuilding Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 52-53. "Jinmyocho Kosho Saiko" states that the enshrined deity is the same as {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|ja|志等美神社}}. The {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} faces east, and the {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}} is located immediately to the right after entering Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 334. Built in {{ill|Shimmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with wooden planks, it is surrounded by a {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} . There is no offering box. == History == [[File:Kiyonoibajinja sengu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Scene from the reconstruction on October 11, 2017.]] The name "Kiyonoiba" refers to the {{ill|wilderness|ja|原野}} around the shrine , while "Iba" means {{ill|weir|ja|堰}} . Over different {{ill|eras|ja|時代}} and {{ill|texts|ja|書物}}, "Iba" has been read as "iba," "iniwa," or "ite" . The shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, meaning it predates 927. It also appears in the "Toyukemiya Gishikicho" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 331, indicating its existence before 804. Rituals ceased by the late {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世}}, and the site was forgotten until {{ill|Oomiya-ji|ja|大宮司}} {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長}} restored it on June 21, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: July 25, 1663) Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 329-332. During {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, the shrine was damaged in the {{ill|Ujiyamada Air Raid|ja|宇治山田空襲}} on July 29, 1945 Ise City (1968): p. 445. Postwar {{ill|land readjustment|ja|区画整理}} reduced the northern area and expanded the western part . The shrine was rebuilt on May 10, 1980 and again on October 11, 2017. == Rituals == {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November) are conducted by a {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}} at the shrine. {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}} (January 3), {{ill|National Foundation Day|ja|建国記念祭}} (February 11), {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭}} (February 23), and {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja|風日祈祭}} (May, August) are held remotely Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 332. == Flora == The precinct has tall trees such as {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ}} and {{ill|camphor trees|ja|クスノキ}}, along with mid-sized trees like {{ill|arakashi|ja|アラカシ}} and {{ill|zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} . Newly added western areas have {{ill|coral trees|ja|サンゴジュ}} . == Access == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}}: About 300m southeast of {{ill|Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|ja|山田上口駅}} . * {{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道}} {{ill|Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}: Around 5 minutes' walk (450m) from {{ill|Miyamachi Station|ja|宮町駅}}. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} {{ill|Bus|ja|路線バス}}: 3-minute walk (300m) from "Miyamachi" {{ill|bus stop|ja|バス停留所}}. ; By Car * {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}: About 8 minutes (3.35 km) north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mikimoto Road). [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:清野井庭神社]] == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史』伊勢市役所、昭和43年3月31日、954p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年3月5日、1690p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0601.html 清野井庭神社（きよのいばじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|きよのいはしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category: :qq]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]][[Category:Ugoodbye $1]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kiyonoiba Shrine-qq]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]][[ja:清野井庭神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:清野井庭神社]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:qq]]
