{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kiyonoiba-jinja 02.jpg|270px]]
Shrine Hall | location = 1-8 Tokiwachō, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|42|N|136|41|48.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Kayanohime|ja|草野姫命}} | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor rank), {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Before [[Enryaku era|Enryaku]] 23 (804 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[Kinen-sai]] (Prayer Festival), [[Tsukinami-sai]] (Monthly Festival), among others }} '''{{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社}}''' (*Kiyonoiba Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It ranks 9th among the auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): Volume 1, p. 12. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}} Tokiwa 1-chome, 1197 Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 329 (in the residential address system: Tokiwa 1-chome, 8) is where the shrine is located. There is a theory that the former shrine site was at the {{ill|Ise Takayanagi Shopping Street|ja|伊勢高柳商店街}} in Miyamachi 1-chome, Ise City . If this theory is correct, it would suggest a different position for {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社}} and {{ill|Oma Kokuso Shrine|ja|大間国生神社}} as described in "Ruiju Jingihongen" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 330. The "Ujiyamada City History" suggests that the original Oma Kokuso Shrine had a larger precinct and that Kiyonoiba Shrine was established on the current site based on the situation during its reconstruction Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 936-937. The shrine grounds are rectangular, with an area of 1,095 square meters Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 333. The {{ill|enshrined deity|ja|deity}} is {{ill|Kayanohime|ja|カヤノヒメ}} (Kayanohime no Mikoto Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 52). The deity is associated with the {{ill|irrigation water|ja|用水路}} near the shrine, though another theory suggests it is a {{ill|spirit|ja|分霊}} of the deity of shipbuilding Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 52-53. "Jinmyocho Kosho Saiko" states that the enshrined deity is the same as {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|ja|志等美神社}}. The {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} faces east, and the {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}} is located immediately to the right after entering Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 334. Built in {{ill|Shimmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with wooden planks, it is surrounded by a {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} . There is no offering box. == History == [[File:Kiyonoibajinja sengu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Scene from the reconstruction on October 11, 2017.]] The name "Kiyonoiba" refers to the {{ill|wilderness|ja|原野}} around the shrine , while "Iba" means {{ill|weir|ja|堰}} . Over different {{ill|eras|ja|時代}} and {{ill|texts|ja|書物}}, "Iba" has been read as "iba," "iniwa," or "ite" . The shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, meaning it predates 927. It also appears in the "Toyukemiya Gishikicho" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 331, indicating its existence before 804. Rituals ceased by the late {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世}}, and the site was forgotten until {{ill|Oomiya-ji|ja|大宮司}} {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長}} restored it on June 21, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: July 25, 1663) Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 329-332. During {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, the shrine was damaged in the {{ill|Ujiyamada Air Raid|ja|宇治山田空襲}} on July 29, 1945 Ise City (1968): p. 445. Postwar {{ill|land readjustment|ja|区画整理}} reduced the northern area and expanded the western part . The shrine was rebuilt on May 10, 1980 and again on October 11, 2017. == Rituals == {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November) are conducted by a {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}} at the shrine. {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}} (January 3), {{ill|National Foundation Day|ja|建国記念祭}} (February 11), {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭}} (February 23), and {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja|風日祈祭}} (May, August) are held remotely Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 332. == Flora == The precinct has tall trees such as {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ}} and {{ill|camphor trees|ja|クスノキ}}, along with mid-sized trees like {{ill|arakashi|ja|アラカシ}} and {{ill|zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} . Newly added western areas have {{ill|coral trees|ja|サンゴジュ}} . == Access == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}}: About 300m southeast of {{ill|Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|ja|山田上口駅}} . * {{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道}} {{ill|Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}: Around 5 minutes' walk (450m) from {{ill|Miyamachi Station|ja|宮町駅}}. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} {{ill|Bus|ja|路線バス}}: 3-minute walk (300m) from "Miyamachi" {{ill|bus stop|ja|バス停留所}}. ; By Car * {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}: About 8 minutes (3.35 km) north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mikimoto Road). [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:清野井庭神社]] == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史』伊勢市役所、昭和43年3月31日、954p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年3月5日、1690p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0601.html 清野井庭神社（きよのいばじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]] [[Category:11 deprecated open]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]][[ja:清野井庭神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]]

The shrine was rebuilt on May 10, 1980 and again on October 11, 2017. == Rituals == {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November) are conducted by a {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}} at the shrine. {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}} (January 3), {{ill|National Foundation Day|ja|建国記念祭}} (February 11), {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭}} (February 23), and {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja|風日祈祭}} (May, August) are held remotely Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 332. == Flora == The precinct has tall trees such as {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ}} and {{ill|camphor trees|ja|クスノキ}}, along with mid-sized trees like {{ill|arakashi|ja|アラカシ}} and {{ill|zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} . Newly added western areas have {{ill|coral trees|ja|サンゴジュ}} . == Access == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}}: About 300m southeast of {{ill|Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|ja|山田上口駅}} . * {{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道}} {{ill|Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}: Around 5 minutes' walk (450m) from {{ill|Miyamachi Station|ja|宮町駅}}. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} {{ill|Bus|ja|路線バス}}: 3-minute walk (300m) from "Miyamachi" {{ill|bus stop|ja|バス停留所}}. ; By Car * {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}: About 8 minutes (3.35 km) north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mikimoto Road). [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:清野井庭神社]] == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史』伊勢市役所、昭和43年3月31日、954p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年3月5日、1690p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0601.html 清野井庭神社（きよのいばじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]] [[Category:11 deprecated open]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]][[ja:清野井庭神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kiyonoiba-jinja 02.jpg|270px]]
Shrine Hall | location = 1-8 Tokiwachō, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|42|N|136|41|48.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Kayanohime|ja|草野姫命}} | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor rank), {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Before [[Enryaku era|Enryaku]] 23 (804 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[Kinen-sai]] (Prayer Festival), [[Tsukinami-sai]] (Monthly Festival), among others }} '''{{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社}}''' (*Kiyonoiba Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It ranks 9th among the auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): Volume 1, p. 12. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}} Tokiwa 1-chome, 1197 Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 329 (in the residential address system: Tokiwa 1-chome, 8) is where the shrine is located. There is a theory that the former shrine site was at the {{ill|Ise Takayanagi Shopping Street|ja|伊勢高柳商店街}} in Miyamachi 1-chome, Ise City . If this theory is correct, it would suggest a different position for {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社}} and {{ill|Oma Kokuso Shrine|ja|大間国生神社}} as described in "Ruiju Jingihongen" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 330. The "Ujiyamada City History" suggests that the original Oma Kokuso Shrine had a larger precinct and that Kiyonoiba Shrine was established on the current site based on the situation during its reconstruction Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 936-937. The shrine grounds are rectangular, with an area of 1,095 square meters Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 333. The {{ill|enshrined deity|ja|deity}} is {{ill|Kayanohime|ja|カヤノヒメ}} (Kayanohime no Mikoto Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 52). The deity is associated with the {{ill|irrigation water|ja|用水路}} near the shrine, though another theory suggests it is a {{ill|spirit|ja|分霊}} of the deity of shipbuilding Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 52-53. "Jinmyocho Kosho Saiko" states that the enshrined deity is the same as {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|ja|志等美神社}}. The {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} faces east, and the {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}} is located immediately to the right after entering Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 334. Built in {{ill|Shimmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with wooden planks, it is surrounded by a {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} . There is no offering box. == History == [[File:Kiyonoibajinja sengu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Scene from the reconstruction on October 11, 2017.]] The name "Kiyonoiba" refers to the {{ill|wilderness|ja|原野}} around the shrine , while "Iba" means {{ill|weir|ja|堰}} . Over different {{ill|eras|ja|時代}} and {{ill|texts|ja|書物}}, "Iba" has been read as "iba," "iniwa," or "ite" . The shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, meaning it predates 927. It also appears in the "Toyukemiya Gishikicho" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 331, indicating its existence before 804. Rituals ceased by the late {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世}}, and the site was forgotten until {{ill|Oomiya-ji|ja|大宮司}} {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長}} restored it on June 21, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: July 25, 1663) Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 329-332. During {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, the shrine was damaged in the {{ill|Ujiyamada Air Raid|ja|宇治山田空襲}} on July 29, 1945 Ise City (1968): p. 445. Postwar {{ill|land readjustment|ja|区画整理}} reduced the northern area and expanded the western part . The shrine was rebuilt on May 10, 1980 and again on October 11, 2017. == Rituals == {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November) are conducted by a {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}} at the shrine. {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}} (January 3), {{ill|National Foundation Day|ja|建国記念祭}} (February 11), {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭}} (February 23), and {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja|風日祈祭}} (May, August) are held remotely Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 332. == Flora == The precinct has tall trees such as {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ}} and {{ill|camphor trees|ja|クスノキ}}, along with mid-sized trees like {{ill|arakashi|ja|アラカシ}} and {{ill|zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} . Newly added western areas have {{ill|coral trees|ja|サンゴジュ}} . == Access == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}}: About 300m southeast of {{ill|Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|ja|山田上口駅}} . * {{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道}} {{ill|Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}: Around 5 minutes' walk (450m) from {{ill|Miyamachi Station|ja|宮町駅}}. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} {{ill|Bus|ja|路線バス}}: 3-minute walk (300m) from "Miyamachi" {{ill|bus stop|ja|バス停留所}}. ; By Car * {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}: About 8 minutes (3.35 km) north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mikimoto Road). [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:清野井庭神社]] == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史』伊勢市役所、昭和43年3月31日、954p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年3月5日、1690p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0601.html 清野井庭神社（きよのいばじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]][[ja:清野井庭神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]]

There is a theory that the former shrine site was at the {{ill|Ise Takayanagi Shopping Street|ja|伊勢高柳商店街}} in Miyamachi 1-chome, Ise City . If this theory is correct, it would suggest a different position for {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社}} and {{ill|Oma Kokuso Shrine|ja|大間国生神社}} as described in "Ruiju Jingihongen" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 330. The "Ujiyamada City History" suggests that the original Oma Kokuso Shrine had a larger precinct and that Kiyonoiba Shrine was established on the current site based on the situation during its reconstruction Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 936-937. The shrine grounds are rectangular, with an area of 1,095 square meters Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 333. The {{ill|enshrined deity|ja|deity}} is {{ill|Kayanohime|ja|カヤノヒメ}} (Kayanohime no Mikoto Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 52). The deity is associated with the {{ill|irrigation water|ja|用水路}} near the shrine, though another theory suggests it is a {{ill|spirit|ja|分霊}} of the deity of shipbuilding Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 52-53. "Jinmyocho Kosho Saiko" states that the enshrined deity is the same as {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|ja|志等美神社}}. The {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} faces east, and the {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}} is located immediately to the right after entering Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 334. Built in {{ill|Shimmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with wooden planks, it is surrounded by a {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} . There is no offering box. == History == [[File:Kiyonoibajinja sengu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Scene from the reconstruction on October 11, 2017.]] The name "Kiyonoiba" refers to the {{ill|wilderness|ja|原野}} around the shrine , while "Iba" means {{ill|weir|ja|堰}} . Over different {{ill|eras|ja|時代}} and {{ill|texts|ja|書物}}, "Iba" has been read as "iba," "iniwa," or "ite" . The shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, meaning it predates 927. It also appears in the "Toyukemiya Gishikicho" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 331, indicating its existence before 804. Rituals ceased by the late {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世}}, and the site was forgotten until {{ill|Oomiya-ji|ja|大宮司}} {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長}} restored it on June 21, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: July 25, 1663) Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 329-332. During {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, the shrine was damaged in the {{ill|Ujiyamada Air Raid|ja|宇治山田空襲}} on July 29, 1945 Ise City (1968): p. 445. Postwar {{ill|land readjustment|ja|区画整理}} reduced the northern area and expanded the western part . The shrine was rebuilt on May 10, 1980 and again on October 11, 2017. == Rituals == {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November) are conducted by a {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}} at the shrine. {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}} (January 3), {{ill|National Foundation Day|ja|建国記念祭}} (February 11), {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭}} (February 23), and {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja|風日祈祭}} (May, August) are held remotely Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 332. == Flora == The precinct has tall trees such as {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ}} and {{ill|camphor trees|ja|クスノキ}}, along with mid-sized trees like {{ill|arakashi|ja|アラカシ}} and {{ill|zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} . Newly added western areas have {{ill|coral trees|ja|サンゴジュ}} . == Access == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}}: About 300m southeast of {{ill|Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|ja|山田上口駅}} . * {{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道}} {{ill|Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}: Around 5 minutes' walk (450m) from {{ill|Miyamachi Station|ja|宮町駅}}. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} {{ill|Bus|ja|路線バス}}: 3-minute walk (300m) from "Miyamachi" {{ill|bus stop|ja|バス停留所}}. ; By Car * {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}: About 8 minutes (3.35 km) north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mikimoto Road). [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:清野井庭神社]] == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史』伊勢市役所、昭和43年3月31日、954p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年3月5日、1690p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4== See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0601.html 清野井庭神社（きよのいばじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]][[ja:清野井庭神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]]

ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also! == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0601.html 清野井庭神社（きよのいばじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]][[ja:清野井庭神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also! ==" to "== See Also =="
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kiyonoiba-jinja 02.jpg|270px]]
Shrine Hall | location = 1-8 Tokiwachō, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|42|N|136|41|48.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Kayanohime|ja|草野姫命}} | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor rank), {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Before [[Enryaku era|Enryaku]] 23 (804 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[Kinen-sai]] (Prayer Festival), [[Tsukinami-sai]] (Monthly Festival), among others }} '''{{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社}}''' (*Kiyonoiba Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It ranks 9th among the auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): Volume 1, p. 12. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}} Tokiwa 1-chome, 1197 Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 329 (in the residential address system: Tokiwa 1-chome, 8) is where the shrine is located. There is a theory that the former shrine site was at the {{ill|Ise Takayanagi Shopping Street|ja|伊勢高柳商店街}} in Miyamachi 1-chome, Ise City . If this theory is correct, it would suggest a different position for {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社}} and {{ill|Oma Kokuso Shrine|ja|大間国生神社}} as described in "Ruiju Jingihongen" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 330. The "Ujiyamada City History" suggests that the original Oma Kokuso Shrine had a larger precinct and that Kiyonoiba Shrine was established on the current site based on the situation during its reconstruction Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 936-937. The shrine grounds are rectangular, with an area of 1,095 square meters Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 333. The {{ill|enshrined deity|ja|deity}} is {{ill|Kayanohime|ja|カヤノヒメ}} (Kayanohime no Mikoto Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 52). The deity is associated with the {{ill|irrigation water|ja|用水路}} near the shrine, though another theory suggests it is a {{ill|spirit|ja|分霊}} of the deity of shipbuilding Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 52-53. "Jinmyocho Kosho Saiko" states that the enshrined deity is the same as {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|ja|志等美神社}}. The {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} faces east, and the {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}} is located immediately to the right after entering Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 334. Built in {{ill|Shimmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with wooden planks, it is surrounded by a {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} . There is no offering box. == History == [[File:Kiyonoibajinja sengu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Scene from the reconstruction on October 11, 2017.]] The name "Kiyonoiba" refers to the {{ill|wilderness|ja|原野}} around the shrine , while "Iba" means {{ill|weir|ja|堰}} . Over different {{ill|eras|ja|時代}} and {{ill|texts|ja|書物}}, "Iba" has been read as "iba," "iniwa," or "ite" . The shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, meaning it predates 927. It also appears in the "Toyukemiya Gishikicho" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 331, indicating its existence before 804. Rituals ceased by the late {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世}}, and the site was forgotten until {{ill|Oomiya-ji|ja|大宮司}} {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長}} restored it on June 21, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: July 25, 1663) Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 329-332. During {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, the shrine was damaged in the {{ill|Ujiyamada Air Raid|ja|宇治山田空襲}} on July 29, 1945 Ise City (1968): p. 445. Postwar {{ill|land readjustment|ja|区画整理}} reduced the northern area and expanded the western part . The shrine was rebuilt on May 10, 1980 and again on October 11, 2017. == Rituals == {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November) are conducted by a {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}} at the shrine. {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}} (January 3), {{ill|National Foundation Day|ja|建国記念祭}} (February 11), {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭}} (February 23), and {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja|風日祈祭}} (May, August) are held remotely Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 332. == Flora == The precinct has tall trees such as {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ}} and {{ill|camphor trees|ja|クスノキ}}, along with mid-sized trees like {{ill|arakashi|ja|アラカシ}} and {{ill|zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} . Newly added western areas have {{ill|coral trees|ja|サンゴジュ}} . == Access == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}}: About 300m southeast of {{ill|Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|ja|山田上口駅}} . * {{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道}} {{ill|Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}: Around 5 minutes' walk (450m) from {{ill|Miyamachi Station|ja|宮町駅}}. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} {{ill|Bus|ja|路線バス}}: 3-minute walk (300m) from "Miyamachi" {{ill|bus stop|ja|バス停留所}}. ; By Car * {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}: About 8 minutes (3.35 km) north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mikimoto Road). [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:清野井庭神社]] == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史』伊勢市役所、昭和43年3月31日、954p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年3月5日、1690p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0601.html 清野井庭神社（きよのいばじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]][[ja:清野井庭神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

During {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, the shrine was damaged in the {{ill|Ujiyamada Air Raid|ja|宇治山田空襲}} on July 29, 1945 Ise City (1968): p. 445. Postwar {{ill|land readjustment|ja|区画整理}} reduced the northern area and expanded the western part . The shrine was rebuilt on May 10, 1980 and again on October 11, 2017. == Rituals == {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November) are conducted by a {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}} at the shrine. {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}} (January 3), {{ill|National Foundation Day|ja|建国記念祭}} (February 11), {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭}} (February 23), and {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja|風日祈祭}} (May, August) are held remotely Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 332. == Flora == The precinct has tall trees such as {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ}} and {{ill|camphor trees|ja|クスノキ}}, along with mid-sized trees like {{ill|arakashi|ja|アラカシ}} and {{ill|zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} . Newly added western areas have {{ill|coral trees|ja|サンゴジュ}} . == Access == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}}: About 300m southeast of {{ill|Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|ja|山田上口駅}} . * {{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道}} {{ill|Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}: Around 5 minutes' walk (450m) from {{ill|Miyamachi Station|ja|宮町駅}}. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} {{ill|Bus|ja|路線バス}}: 3-minute walk (300m) from "Miyamachi" {{ill|bus stop|ja|バス停留所}}. ; By Car * {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}: About 8 minutes (3.35 km) north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mikimoto Road). [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:清野井庭神社]] == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史』伊勢市役所、昭和43rd year 3月31日、954p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年3月5日、1690p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0601.html 清野井庭神社（きよのいばじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]][[ja:清野井庭神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "4年" to "4th year "
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kiyonoiba-jinja 02.jpg|270px]]
Shrine Hall | location = 1-8 Tokiwachō, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|42|N|136|41|48.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Kayanohime|ja|草野姫命}} | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor rank), {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Before [[Enryaku era|Enryaku]] 23 (804 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[Kinen-sai]] (Prayer Festival), [[Tsukinami-sai]] (Monthly Festival), among others }} '''{{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社}}''' (*Kiyonoiba Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It ranks 9th among the auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): Volume 1, p. 12. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}} Tokiwa 1-chome, 1197 Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 329 (in the residential address system: Tokiwa 1-chome, 8) is where the shrine is located. There is a theory that the former shrine site was at the {{ill|Ise Takayanagi Shopping Street|ja|伊勢高柳商店街}} in Miyamachi 1-chome, Ise City . If this theory is correct, it would suggest a different position for {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社}} and {{ill|Oma Kokuso Shrine|ja|大間国生神社}} as described in "Ruiju Jingihongen" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 330. The "Ujiyamada City History" suggests that the original Oma Kokuso Shrine had a larger precinct and that Kiyonoiba Shrine was established on the current site based on the situation during its reconstruction Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 936-937. The shrine grounds are rectangular, with an area of 1,095 square meters Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 333. The {{ill|enshrined deity|ja|deity}} is {{ill|Kayanohime|ja|カヤノヒメ}} (Kayanohime no Mikoto Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 52). The deity is associated with the {{ill|irrigation water|ja|用水路}} near the shrine, though another theory suggests it is a {{ill|spirit|ja|分霊}} of the deity of shipbuilding Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 52-53. "Jinmyocho Kosho Saiko" states that the enshrined deity is the same as {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|ja|志等美神社}}. The {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} faces east, and the {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}} is located immediately to the right after entering Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 334. Built in {{ill|Shimmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with wooden planks, it is surrounded by a {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} . There is no offering box. == History == [[File:Kiyonoibajinja sengu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Scene from the reconstruction on October 11, 2017.]] The name "Kiyonoiba" refers to the {{ill|wilderness|ja|原野}} around the shrine , while "Iba" means {{ill|weir|ja|堰}} . Over different {{ill|eras|ja|時代}} and {{ill|texts|ja|書物}}, "Iba" has been read as "iba," "iniwa," or "ite" . The shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, meaning it predates 927. It also appears in the "Toyukemiya Gishikicho" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 331, indicating its existence before 804. Rituals ceased by the late {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世}}, and the site was forgotten until {{ill|Oomiya-ji|ja|大宮司}} {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長}} restored it on June 21, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: July 25, 1663) Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 329-332. During {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, the shrine was damaged in the {{ill|Ujiyamada Air Raid|ja|宇治山田空襲}} on July 29, 1945 Ise City (1968): p. 445. Postwar {{ill|land readjustment|ja|区画整理}} reduced the northern area and expanded the western part . The shrine was rebuilt on May 10, 1980 and again on October 11, 2017. == Rituals == {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November) are conducted by a {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}} at the shrine. {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}} (January 3), {{ill|National Foundation Day|ja|建国記念祭}} (February 11), {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭}} (February 23), and {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja|風日祈祭}} (May, August) are held remotely Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 332. == Flora == The precinct has tall trees such as {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ}} and {{ill|camphor trees|ja|クスノキ}}, along with mid-sized trees like {{ill|arakashi|ja|アラカシ}} and {{ill|zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} . Newly added western areas have {{ill|coral trees|ja|サンゴジュ}} . == Access == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}}: About 300m southeast of {{ill|Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|ja|山田上口駅}} . * {{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道}} {{ill|Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}: Around 5 minutes' walk (450m) from {{ill|Miyamachi Station|ja|宮町駅}}. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} {{ill|Bus|ja|路線バス}}: 3-minute walk (300m) from "Miyamachi" {{ill|bus stop|ja|バス停留所}}. ; By Car * {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}: About 8 minutes (3.35 km) north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mikimoto Road). [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:清野井庭神社]] == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史』伊勢市役所、昭和43rd year 3月31日、954p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 3月5日、1690p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0601.html 清野井庭神社（きよのいばじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]][[ja:清野井庭神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kiyonoiba-jinja 02.jpg|270px]]
Shrine Hall | location = 1-8 Tokiwachō, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|42|N|136|41|48.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Kayanohime|ja|草野姫命}} | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor rank), {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Before [[Enryaku era|Enryaku]] 23 (804 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[Kinen-sai]] (Prayer Festival), [[Tsukinami-sai]] (Monthly Festival), among others }} '''{{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社}}''' (*Kiyonoiba Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It ranks 9th among the auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): Volume 1, p. 12. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}} Tokiwa 1-chome, 1197 Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 329 (in the residential address system: Tokiwa 1-chome, 8) is where the shrine is located. There is a theory that the former shrine site was at the {{ill|Ise Takayanagi Shopping Street|ja|伊勢高柳商店街}} in Miyamachi 1-chome, Ise City . If this theory is correct, it would suggest a different position for {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社}} and {{ill|Oma Kokuso Shrine|ja|大間国生神社}} as described in "Ruiju Jingihongen" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 330. The "Ujiyamada City History" suggests that the original Oma Kokuso Shrine had a larger precinct and that Kiyonoiba Shrine was established on the current site based on the situation during its reconstruction Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 936-937. The shrine grounds are rectangular, with an area of 1,095 square meters Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 333. The {{ill|enshrined deity|ja|deity}} is {{ill|Kayanohime|ja|カヤノヒメ}} (Kayanohime no Mikoto Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 52). The deity is associated with the {{ill|irrigation water|ja|用水路}} near the shrine, though another theory suggests it is a {{ill|spirit|ja|分霊}} of the deity of shipbuilding Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 52-53. "Jinmyocho Kosho Saiko" states that the enshrined deity is the same as {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|ja|志等美神社}}. The {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} faces east, and the {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}} is located immediately to the right after entering Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 334. Built in {{ill|Shimmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with wooden planks, it is surrounded by a {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} . There is no offering box. == History == [[File:Kiyonoibajinja sengu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Scene from the reconstruction on October 11, 2017.]] The name "Kiyonoiba" refers to the {{ill|wilderness|ja|原野}} around the shrine , while "Iba" means {{ill|weir|ja|堰}} . Over different {{ill|eras|ja|時代}} and {{ill|texts|ja|書物}}, "Iba" has been read as "iba," "iniwa," or "ite" . The shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, meaning it predates 927. It also appears in the "Toyukemiya Gishikicho" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 331, indicating its existence before 804. Rituals ceased by the late {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世}}, and the site was forgotten until {{ill|Oomiya-ji|ja|大宮司}} {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長}} restored it on June 21, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: July 25, 1663) Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 329-332. During {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, the shrine was damaged in the {{ill|Ujiyamada Air Raid|ja|宇治山田空襲}} on July 29, 1945 Ise City (1968): p. 445. Postwar {{ill|land readjustment|ja|区画整理}} reduced the northern area and expanded the western part . The shrine was rebuilt on May 10, 1980 and again on October 11, 2017. == Rituals == {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November) are conducted by a {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}} at the shrine. {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}} (January 3), {{ill|National Foundation Day|ja|建国記念祭}} (February 11), {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭}} (February 23), and {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja|風日祈祭}} (May, August) are held remotely Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 332. == Flora == The precinct has tall trees such as {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ}} and {{ill|camphor trees|ja|クスノキ}}, along with mid-sized trees like {{ill|arakashi|ja|アラカシ}} and {{ill|zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} . Newly added western areas have {{ill|coral trees|ja|サンゴジュ}} . == Access == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}}: About 300m southeast of {{ill|Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|ja|山田上口駅}} . * {{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道}} {{ill|Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}: Around 5 minutes' walk (450m) from {{ill|Miyamachi Station|ja|宮町駅}}. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} {{ill|Bus|ja|路線バス}}: 3-minute walk (300m) from "Miyamachi" {{ill|bus stop|ja|バス停留所}}. ; By Car * {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}: About 8 minutes (3.35 km) north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mikimoto Road). [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:清野井庭神社]] == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史』伊勢市役所、昭和43rd year 3月31日、954p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 3月5日、1690p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0601.html 清野井庭神社（きよのいばじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]][[ja:清野井庭神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

[[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:清野井庭神社]] == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史』伊勢市役所、昭和43rd year 3月31日、954p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 3月5日、1690p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0601.html 清野井庭神社（きよのいばじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]][[ja:清野井庭神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:New]]

revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kiyonoiba-jinja 02.jpg|270px]]
Shrine Hall | location = 1-8 Tokiwachō, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|42|N|136|41|48.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Kayanohime|ja|草野姫命}} | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor rank), {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Before [[Enryaku era|Enryaku]] 23 (804 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[Kinen-sai]] (Prayer Festival), [[Tsukinami-sai]] (Monthly Festival), among others }} '''{{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社}}''' (*Kiyonoiba Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It ranks 9th among the auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): Volume 1, p. 12. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}} Tokiwa 1-chome, 1197 Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 329 (in the residential address system: Tokiwa 1-chome, 8) is where the shrine is located. There is a theory that the former shrine site was at the {{ill|Ise Takayanagi Shopping Street|ja|伊勢高柳商店街}} in Miyamachi 1-chome, Ise City . If this theory is correct, it would suggest a different position for {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社}} and {{ill|Oma Kokuso Shrine|ja|大間国生神社}} as described in "Ruiju Jingihongen" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 330. The "Ujiyamada City History" suggests that the original Oma Kokuso Shrine had a larger precinct and that Kiyonoiba Shrine was established on the current site based on the situation during its reconstruction Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 936-937. The shrine grounds are rectangular, with an area of 1,095 square meters Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 333. The {{ill|enshrined deity|ja|deity}} is {{ill|Kayanohime|ja|カヤノヒメ}} (Kayanohime no Mikoto Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 52). The deity is associated with the {{ill|irrigation water|ja|用水路}} near the shrine, though another theory suggests it is a {{ill|spirit|ja|分霊}} of the deity of shipbuilding Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 52-53. "Jinmyocho Kosho Saiko" states that the enshrined deity is the same as {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|ja|志等美神社}}. The {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} faces east, and the {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}} is located immediately to the right after entering Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 334. Built in {{ill|Shimmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with wooden planks, it is surrounded by a {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} . There is no offering box. == History == [[File:Kiyonoibajinja sengu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Scene from the reconstruction on October 11, 2017.]] The name "Kiyonoiba" refers to the {{ill|wilderness|ja|原野}} around the shrine , while "Iba" means {{ill|weir|ja|堰}} . Over different {{ill|eras|ja|時代}} and {{ill|texts|ja|書物}}, "Iba" has been read as "iba," "iniwa," or "ite" . The shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, meaning it predates 927. It also appears in the "Toyukemiya Gishikicho" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 331, indicating its existence before 804. Rituals ceased by the late {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世}}, and the site was forgotten until {{ill|Oomiya-ji|ja|大宮司}} {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長}} restored it on June 21, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: July 25, 1663) Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 329-332. During {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, the shrine was damaged in the {{ill|Ujiyamada Air Raid|ja|宇治山田空襲}} on July 29, 1945 Ise City (1968): p. 445. Postwar {{ill|land readjustment|ja|区画整理}} reduced the northern area and expanded the western part . The shrine was rebuilt on May 10, 1980 and again on October 11, 2017. == Rituals == {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November) are conducted by a {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜}} at the shrine. {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}} (January 3), {{ill|National Foundation Day|ja|建国記念祭}} (February 11), {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭}} (February 23), and {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja|風日祈祭}} (May, August) are held remotely Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 332. == Flora == The precinct has tall trees such as {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ}} and {{ill|camphor trees|ja|クスノキ}}, along with mid-sized trees like {{ill|arakashi|ja|アラカシ}} and {{ill|zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} . Newly added western areas have {{ill|coral trees|ja|サンゴジュ}} . == Access == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}}: About 300m southeast of {{ill|Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|ja|山田上口駅}} . * {{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道}} {{ill|Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}: Around 5 minutes' walk (450m) from {{ill|Miyamachi Station|ja|宮町駅}}. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} {{ill|Bus|ja|路線バス}}: 3-minute walk (300m) from "Miyamachi" {{ill|bus stop|ja|バス停留所}}. ; By Car * {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}: About 8 minutes (3.35 km) north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mikimoto Road). == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史』伊勢市役所、昭和43rd year 3月31日、954p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 3月5日、1690p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0601.html 清野井庭神社（きよのいばじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[ja:清野井庭神社]] [[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]]

The "Ujiyamada City History" suggests that the original Oma Kokuso Shrine had a larger precinct and that Kiyonoiba Shrine was established on the current site based on the situation during its reconstruction Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 936-937. The shrine grounds are rectangular, with an area of 1,095 square meters Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 333. The {{ill|enshrined deity|ja|deity|12=draft}} is {{ill|Kayanohime|ja|カヤノヒメ|12=draft}} (Kayanohime no Mikoto Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 52). The deity is associated with the {{ill|irrigation water|ja|用水路|12=draft}} near the shrine, though another theory suggests it is a {{ill|spirit|ja|分霊|12=draft}} of the deity of shipbuilding Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 52-53. "Jinmyocho Kosho Saiko" states that the enshrined deity is the same as {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|ja|志等美神社|12=draft}}. The {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|12=draft}} faces east, and the {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=draft}} is located immediately to the right after entering Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 334. Built in {{ill|Shimmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft}} style with wooden planks, it is surrounded by a {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft}} . There is no offering box. == History == [[File:Kiyonoibajinja sengu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Scene from the reconstruction on October 11, 2017.]] The name "Kiyonoiba" refers to the {{ill|wilderness|ja|原野|12=draft}} around the shrine , while "Iba" means {{ill|weir|ja|堰|12=draft}} . Over different {{ill|eras|ja|時代|12=draft}} and {{ill|texts|ja|書物|12=draft}}, "Iba" has been read as "iba," "iniwa," or "ite" . The shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}, meaning it predates 927. It also appears in the "Toyukemiya Gishikicho" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 331, indicating its existence before 804. Rituals ceased by the late {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世|12=draft}}, and the site was forgotten until {{ill|Oomiya-ji|ja|大宮司|12=draft}} {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|12=draft}} restored it on June 21, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: July 25, 1663) Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 329-332. During {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦|12=draft}}, the shrine was damaged in the {{ill|Ujiyamada Air Raid|ja|宇治山田空襲|12=draft}} on July 29, 1945 Ise City (1968): p. 445. Postwar {{ill|land readjustment|ja|区画整理|12=draft}} reduced the northern area and expanded the western part . The shrine was rebuilt on May 10, 1980 and again on October 11, 2017. == Rituals == {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=draft}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|12=draft}} (November) are conducted by a {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜|12=draft}} at the shrine. {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=draft}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=draft}} (January 3), {{ill|National Foundation Day|ja|建国記念祭|12=draft}} (February 11), {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=draft}} (February 23), and {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja|風日祈祭|12=draft}} (May, August) are held remotely Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 332. == Flora == The precinct has tall trees such as {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ|12=draft}} and {{ill|camphor trees|ja|クスノキ|12=draft}}, along with mid-sized trees like {{ill|arakashi|ja|アラカシ|12=draft}} and {{ill|zelkova|ja|ケヤキ|12=draft}} . Newly added western areas have {{ill|coral trees|ja|サンゴジュ|12=draft}} . == Access == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft}}: About 300m southeast of {{ill|Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|ja|山田上口駅|12=draft}} . * {{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft}}: Around 5 minutes' walk (450m) from {{ill|Miyamachi Station|ja|宮町駅|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|12=draft}} {{ill|Bus|ja|路線バス|12=draft}}: 3-minute walk (300m) from "Miyamachi" {{ill|bus stop|ja|バス停留所|12=draft}}. ; By Car * {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft}}: About 8 minutes (3.35 km) north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=draft}} (Mikimoto Road). == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史』伊勢市役所、昭和43rd year 3月31日、954p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 3月5日、1690p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0601.html 清野井庭神社（きよのいばじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[ja:清野井庭神社]] [[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]]

It ranks 9th among the auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): Volume 1, p. 12. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|12=draft}} Tokiwa 1-chome, 1197 Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 329 (in the residential address system: Tokiwa 1-chome, 8) is where the shrine is located. There is a theory that the former shrine site was at the {{ill|Ise Takayanagi Shopping Street|ja|伊勢高柳商店街|12=draft}} in Miyamachi 1-chome, Ise City . If this theory is correct, it would suggest a different position for {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Oma Kokuso Shrine|ja|大間国生神社|12=draft}} as described in "Ruiju Jingihongen" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 330. The {{ill|enshrined deity|ja|deity|12=draft}} is {{ill|Kayanohime|ja|カヤノヒメ|12=draft}} (Kayanohime no Mikoto Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 52). "Jinmyocho Kosho Saiko" states that the enshrined deity is the same as {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|ja|志等美神社|12=draft}}. The {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|12=draft}} faces east, and the {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=draft}} is located immediately to the right after entering Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 334. Built in {{ill|Shimmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft}} style with wooden planks, it is surrounded by a {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft}} . There is no offering box. == History == [[File:Kiyonoibajinja sengu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Scene from the reconstruction on October 11, 2017.]] The name "Kiyonoiba" refers to the {{ill|wilderness|ja|原野|12=draft}} around the shrine , while "Iba" means {{ill|weir|ja|堰|12=draft}} . Over different {{ill|eras|ja|時代|12=draft}} and {{ill|texts|ja|書物|12=draft}}, "Iba" has been read as "iba," "iniwa," or "ite" . The shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}, meaning it predates 927. It also appears in the "Toyukemiya Gishikicho" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 331, indicating its existence before 804. Rituals ceased by the late {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世|12=draft}}, and the site was forgotten until {{ill|Oomiya-ji|ja|大宮司|12=draft}} {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|12=draft}} restored it on June 21, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: July 25, 1663) Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 329-332. During {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦|12=draft}}, the shrine was damaged in the {{ill|Ujiyamada Air Raid|ja|宇治山田空襲|12=draft}} on July 29, 1945 Ise City (1968): p. 445. Postwar {{ill|land readjustment|ja|区画整理|12=draft}} reduced the northern area and expanded the western part . ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0601.html 清野井庭神社（きよのいばじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[ja:清野井庭神社]] [[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]]

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Bump 1-26]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社|12=draft}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kiyonoiba-jinja 02.jpg|270px]]
Shrine Hall | location = 1-8 Tokiwachō, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|42|N|136|41|48.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Kayanohime|ja|草野姫命|12=draft}} | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor rank), {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Before [[Enryaku era|Enryaku]] 23 (804 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[Kinen-sai]] (Prayer Festival), [[Tsukinami-sai]] (Monthly Festival), among others }} '''{{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社|12=draft}}''' (*Kiyonoiba Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}} (the Outer Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It ranks 9th among the auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): Volume 1, p. 12. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|12=draft}} Tokiwa 1-chome, 1197 Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 329 (in the residential address system: Tokiwa 1-chome, 8) is where the shrine is located. There is a theory that the former shrine site was at the {{ill|Ise Takayanagi Shopping Street|ja|伊勢高柳商店街|12=draft}} in Miyamachi 1-chome, Ise City . If this theory is correct, it would suggest a different position for {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Oma Kokuso Shrine|ja|大間国生神社|12=draft}} as described in "Ruiju Jingihongen" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 330. The "Ujiyamada City History" suggests that the original Oma Kokuso Shrine had a larger precinct and that Kiyonoiba Shrine was established on the current site based on the situation during its reconstruction Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 936-937. The shrine grounds are rectangular, with an area of 1,095 square meters Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 333. The {{ill|enshrined deity|ja|deity|12=draft}} is {{ill|Kayanohime|ja|カヤノヒメ|12=draft}} (Kayanohime no Mikoto Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 52). The deity is associated with the {{ill|irrigation water|ja|用水路|12=draft}} near the shrine, though another theory suggests it is a {{ill|spirit|ja|分霊|12=draft}} of the deity of shipbuilding Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 52-53. "Jinmyocho Kosho Saiko" states that the enshrined deity is the same as {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|ja|志等美神社|12=draft}}. The {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|12=draft}} faces east, and the {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=draft}} is located immediately to the right after entering Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 334. Built in {{ill|Shimmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft}} style with wooden planks, it is surrounded by a {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft}} . There is no offering box. == History == [[File:Kiyonoibajinja sengu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Scene from the reconstruction on October 11, 2017.]] The name "Kiyonoiba" refers to the {{ill|wilderness|ja|原野|12=draft}} around the shrine , while "Iba" means {{ill|weir|ja|堰|12=draft}} . Over different {{ill|eras|ja|時代|12=draft}} and {{ill|texts|ja|書物|12=draft}}, "Iba" has been read as "iba," "iniwa," or "ite" . The shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}, meaning it predates 927. It also appears in the "Toyukemiya Gishikicho" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 331, indicating its existence before 804. Rituals ceased by the late {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世|12=draft}}, and the site was forgotten until {{ill|Oomiya-ji|ja|大宮司|12=draft}} {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|12=draft}} restored it on June 21, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: July 25, 1663) Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 329-332. During {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦|12=draft}}, the shrine was damaged in the {{ill|Ujiyamada Air Raid|ja|宇治山田空襲|12=draft}} on July 29, 1945 Ise City (1968): p. 445. Postwar {{ill|land readjustment|ja|区画整理|12=draft}} reduced the northern area and expanded the western part . The shrine was rebuilt on May 10, 1980 and again on October 11, 2017. == Rituals == {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=draft}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|12=draft}} (November) are conducted by a {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜|12=draft}} at the shrine. {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=draft}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=draft}} (January 3), {{ill|National Foundation Day|ja|建国記念祭|12=draft}} (February 11), {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=draft}} (February 23), and {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja|風日祈祭|12=draft}} (May, August) are held remotely Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 332. == Flora == The precinct has tall trees such as {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ|12=draft}} and {{ill|camphor trees|ja|クスノキ|12=draft}}, along with mid-sized trees like {{ill|arakashi|ja|アラカシ|12=draft}} and {{ill|zelkova|ja|ケヤキ|12=draft}} . Newly added western areas have {{ill|coral trees|ja|サンゴジュ|12=draft}} . == Access == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft}}: About 300m southeast of {{ill|Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|ja|山田上口駅|12=draft}} . * {{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft}}: Around 5 minutes' walk (450m) from {{ill|Miyamachi Station|ja|宮町駅|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|12=draft}} {{ill|Bus|ja|路線バス|12=draft}}: 3-minute walk (300m) from "Miyamachi" {{ill|bus stop|ja|バス停留所|12=draft}}. ; By Car * {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft}}: About 8 minutes (3.35 km) north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=draft}} (Mikimoto Road). == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史』伊勢市役所、昭和43rd year 3月31日、954p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 3月5日、1690p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0601.html 清野井庭神社（きよのいばじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[ja:清野井庭神社]] [[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]]

"Jinmyocho Kosho Saiko" states that the enshrined deity is the same as {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|ja|志等美神社|12=draft}}. The {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|12=draft}} faces east, and the {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=draft}} is located immediately to the right after entering Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 334. Built in {{ill|Shimmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft}} style with wooden planks, it is surrounded by a {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft}} . There is no offering box. == History == [[File:Kiyonoibajinja sengu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Scene from the reconstruction on October 11, 2017.]] The name "Kiyonoiba" refers to the {{ill|wilderness|ja|原野|12=draft}} around the shrine , while "Iba" means {{ill|weir|ja|堰|12=draft}} . Over different {{ill|eras|ja|時代|12=draft}} and {{ill|texts|ja|書物|12=draft}}, "Iba" has been read as "iba," "iniwa," or "ite" . The shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}, meaning it predates 927. It also appears in the "Toyukemiya Gishikicho" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 331, indicating its existence before 804. Rituals ceased by the late {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世|12=draft}}, and the site was forgotten until {{ill|Oomiya-ji|ja|大宮司|12=draft}} {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|12=draft}} restored it on June 21, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: July 25, 1663) Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 329-332. During {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦|12=draft}}, the shrine was damaged in the {{ill|Ujiyamada Air Raid|ja|宇治山田空襲|12=draft}} on July 29, 1945 Ise City (1968): p. 445. Postwar {{ill|land readjustment|ja|区画整理|12=draft}} reduced the northern area and expanded the western part . The shrine was rebuilt on May 10, 1980 and again on October 11, 2017. == Rituals == {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=draft}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|12=draft}} (November) are conducted by a {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜|12=draft}} at the shrine. {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=draft}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=draft}} (January 3), {{ill|National Foundation Day|ja|建国記念祭|12=draft}} (February 11), {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=draft}} (February 23), and {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja|風日祈祭|12=draft}} (May, August) are held remotely Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 332. == Flora == The precinct has tall trees such as {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ|12=draft}} and {{ill|camphor trees|ja|クスノキ|12=draft}}, along with mid-sized trees like {{ill|arakashi|ja|アラカシ|12=draft}} and {{ill|zelkova|ja|ケヤキ|12=draft}} . Newly added western areas have {{ill|coral trees|ja|サンゴジュ|12=draft}} . == Access == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft}}: About 300m southeast of {{ill|Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|ja|山田上口駅|12=draft}} . * {{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft}}: Around 5 minutes' walk (450m) from {{ill|Miyamachi Station|ja|宮町駅|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|12=draft}} {{ill|Bus|ja|路線バス|12=draft}}: 3-minute walk (300m) from "Miyamachi" {{ill|bus stop|ja|バス停留所|12=draft}}. ; By Car * {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft}}: About 8 minutes (3.35 km) north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=draft}} (Mikimoto Road). == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史』伊勢市役所、昭和43rd year 3月31日、954p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 3月5日、1690p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0601.html 清野井庭神社（きよのいばじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:清野井庭神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:清野井庭神社]] [[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]]
