During {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/World War II|qq=}}, the shrine was damaged in the {{ill|Ujiyamada Air Raid|ja|宇治山田空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Air Raid|qq=}} on July 29, 1945 Ise City (1968): p. 445. Postwar {{ill|land readjustment|ja|区画整理|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/land readjustment|qq=}} reduced the northern area and expanded the western part . The shrine was rebuilt on May 10, 1980 and again on October 11, 2017. == Rituals == {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinen-sai|qq=}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niinamesai|qq=}} (November) are conducted by a {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi|qq=}} at the shrine. {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} (January 3), {{ill|National Foundation Day|ja|建国記念祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Foundation Day|qq=}} (February 11), {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|qq=}} (February 23), and {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja|風日祈祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|qq=}} (May, August) are held remotely Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 332. == Flora == The precinct has tall trees such as {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enoki|qq=}} and {{ill|camphor trees|ja|クスノキ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/camphor trees|qq=}}, along with mid-sized trees like {{ill|arakashi|ja|アラカシ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/arakashi|qq=}} and {{ill|zelkova|ja|ケヤキ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zelkova|qq=}} . Newly added western areas have {{ill|coral trees|ja|サンゴジュ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/coral trees|qq=}} . == Access == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Central|qq=}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangu Line|qq=}}: About 300m southeast of {{ill|Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|ja|山田上口駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|qq=}} . * {{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu|qq=}} {{ill|Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Line|qq=}}: Around 5 minutes' walk (450m) from {{ill|Miyamachi Station|ja|宮町駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamachi Station|qq=}}. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Kotsu|qq=}} {{ill|Bus|ja|路線バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bus|qq=}}: 3-minute walk (300m) from "Miyamachi" {{ill|bus stop|ja|バス停留所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bus stop|qq=}}. ; By Car * {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Expressway|qq=}}: About 8 minutes (3.35 km) north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Nishi IC|qq=}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|qq=}} (Mikimoto Road). == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史』伊勢市役所、昭和43rd year 3月31日、954p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 3月5日、1690p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0601.html 清野井庭神社（きよのいばじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|清野井庭神社|version=101095922|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] }} [[ja:清野井庭神社]] [[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Engishiki Jinmyocho" to "Engishiki Jinmyōchō"
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyonoiba Shrine|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kiyonoiba-jinja 02.jpg|270px]]
Shrine Hall | location = 1-8 Tokiwachō, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|42|N|136|41|48.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Kayanohime|ja|草野姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanohime|qq=}} | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor Rank), {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Before [[Enryaku era|Enryaku]] 23 (804 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[Kinen-sai]] (Prayer Festival), {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|lt=Tsukinami-sai|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (Monthly Festival), among others }} '''{{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyonoiba Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Kiyonoiba Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}} (the Outer Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks 9th among the auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): Volume 1, p. 12. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise City|qq=}} Tokiwa 1-chome, 1197 Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 329 (in the residential address system: Tokiwa 1-chome, 8) is where the shrine is located. There is a theory that the former shrine site was at the {{ill|Ise Takayanagi Shopping Street|ja|伊勢高柳商店街|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Takayanagi Shopping Street|qq=}} in Miyamachi 1-chome, Ise City . If this theory is correct, it would suggest a different position for {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Oma Kokuso Shrine|ja|大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oma Kokuso Shrine|qq=}} as described in "Ruiju Jingihongen" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 330. The "Ujiyamada City History" suggests that the original Oma Kokuso Shrine had a larger precinct and that Kiyonoiba Shrine was established on the current site based on the situation during its reconstruction Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 936-937. The shrine grounds are rectangular, with an area of 1,095 square meters Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 333. The {{ill|enshrined deity|ja|deity|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|qq=}} is {{ill|Kayanohime|ja|カヤノヒメ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanohime|qq=}} (Kayanohime no Mikoto Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 52). The deity is associated with the {{ill|irrigation water|ja|用水路|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/irrigation water|qq=}} near the shrine, though another theory suggests it is a {{ill|spirit|ja|分霊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/spirit|qq=}} of the deity of shipbuilding Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 52-53. "Jinmyocho Kosho Saiko" states that the enshrined deity is the same as {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|ja|志等美神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitomi Shrine|qq=}}. The {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/torii|qq=}} faces east, and the {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|qq=}} is located immediately to the right after entering Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 334. Built in {{ill|Shimmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimmei-zukuri|qq=}} style with wooden planks, it is surrounded by a {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagaki|qq=}} . There is no offering box. == History == [[File:Kiyonoibajinja sengu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Scene from the reconstruction on October 11, 2017.]] The name "Kiyonoiba" refers to the {{ill|wilderness|ja|原野|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wilderness|qq=}} around the shrine , while "Iba" means {{ill|weir|ja|堰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/weir|qq=}} . Over different {{ill|eras|ja|時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/eras|qq=}} and {{ill|texts|ja|書物|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/texts|qq=}}, "Iba" has been read as "iba," "iniwa," or "ite" . The shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, meaning it predates 927. It also appears in the "Toyukemiya Gishikicho" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 331, indicating its existence before 804. Rituals ceased by the late {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/medieval period|qq=}}, and the site was forgotten until {{ill|Oomiya-ji|ja|大宮司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oomiya-ji|qq=}} {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|qq=}} restored it on June 21, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: July 25, 1663) Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 329-332. During {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/World War II|qq=}}, the shrine was damaged in the {{ill|Ujiyamada Air Raid|ja|宇治山田空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Air Raid|qq=}} on July 29, 1945 Ise City (1968): p. 445. Postwar {{ill|land readjustment|ja|区画整理|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/land readjustment|qq=}} reduced the northern area and expanded the western part . The shrine was rebuilt on May 10, 1980 and again on October 11, 2017. == Rituals == {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinen-sai|qq=}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niinamesai|qq=}} (November) are conducted by a {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi|qq=}} at the shrine. {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} (January 3), {{ill|National Foundation Day|ja|建国記念祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Foundation Day|qq=}} (February 11), {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|qq=}} (February 23), and {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja|風日祈祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|qq=}} (May, August) are held remotely Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 332. == Flora == The precinct has tall trees such as {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enoki|qq=}} and {{ill|camphor trees|ja|クスノキ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/camphor trees|qq=}}, along with mid-sized trees like {{ill|arakashi|ja|アラカシ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/arakashi|qq=}} and {{ill|zelkova|ja|ケヤキ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zelkova|qq=}} . Newly added western areas have {{ill|coral trees|ja|サンゴジュ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/coral trees|qq=}} . == Access == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Central|qq=}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangu Line|qq=}}: About 300m southeast of {{ill|Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|ja|山田上口駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|qq=}} . * {{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu|qq=}} {{ill|Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Line|qq=}}: Around 5 minutes' walk (450m) from {{ill|Miyamachi Station|ja|宮町駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamachi Station|qq=}}. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Kotsu|qq=}} {{ill|Bus|ja|路線バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bus|qq=}}: 3-minute walk (300m) from "Miyamachi" {{ill|bus stop|ja|バス停留所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bus stop|qq=}}. ; By Car * {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Expressway|qq=}}: About 8 minutes (3.35 km) north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Nishi IC|qq=}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|qq=}} (Mikimoto Road). == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史』伊勢市役所、昭和43rd year 3月31日、954p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 3月5日、1690p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0601.html 清野井庭神社（きよのいばじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|清野井庭神社|version=101095922|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] }} [[ja:清野井庭神社]] [[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]]

The deity is associated with the {{ill|irrigation water|ja|用水路|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/irrigation water|qq=}} near the shrine, though another theory suggests it is a {{ill|spirit|ja|分霊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/spirit|qq=}} of the deity of shipbuilding Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 52-53. "Jinmyocho Kosho Saiko" states that the enshrined deity is the same as {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|ja|志等美神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitomi Shrine|qq=}}. The {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/torii|qq=}} faces east, and the {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|qq=}} is located immediately to the right after entering Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 334. Built in {{ill|Shimmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimmei-zukuri|qq=}} style with wooden planks, it is surrounded by a {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagaki|qq=}} . There is no offering box. == History == [[File:Kiyonoibajinja sengu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Scene from the reconstruction on October 11, 2017.]] The name "Kiyonoiba" refers to the {{ill|wilderness|ja|原野|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wilderness|qq=}} around the shrine , while "Iba" means {{ill|weir|ja|堰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/weir|qq=}} . Over different {{ill|eras|ja|時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/eras|qq=}} and {{ill|texts|ja|書物|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/texts|qq=}}, "Iba" has been read as "iba," "iniwa," or "ite" . The shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, meaning it predates 927. It also appears in the "Toyukemiya Gishikicho" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 331, indicating its existence before 804. Rituals ceased by the late {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/medieval period|qq=}}, and the site was forgotten until {{ill|Oomiya-ji|ja|大宮司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oomiya-ji|qq=}} {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|qq=}} restored it on June 21, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: July 25, 1663) Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 329-332. During {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/World War II|qq=}}, the shrine was damaged in the {{ill|Ujiyamada Air Raid|ja|宇治山田空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Air Raid|qq=}} on July 29, 1945 Ise City (1968): p. 445. Postwar {{ill|land readjustment|ja|区画整理|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/land readjustment|qq=}} reduced the northern area and expanded the western part . The shrine was rebuilt on May 10, 1980 and again on October 11, 2017. == Rituals == {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinen-sai|qq=}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niinamesai|qq=}} (November) are conducted by a {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi|qq=}} at the shrine. {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} (January 3), {{ill|National Foundation Day|ja|建国記念祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Foundation Day|qq=}} (February 11), {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|qq=}} (February 23), and {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja|風日祈祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|qq=}} (May, August) are held remotely Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 332. == Flora == The precinct has tall trees such as {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enoki|qq=}} and {{ill|camphor trees|ja|クスノキ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/camphor trees|qq=}}, along with mid-sized trees like {{ill|arakashi|ja|アラカシ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/arakashi|qq=}} and {{ill|zelkova|ja|ケヤキ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zelkova|qq=}} . Newly added western areas have {{ill|coral trees|ja|サンゴジュ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/coral trees|qq=}} . == Access == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Central|qq=}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangu Line|qq=}}: About 300m southeast of {{ill|Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|ja|山田上口駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|qq=}} . * {{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu|qq=}} {{ill|Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Line|qq=}}: Around 5 minutes' walk (450m) from {{ill|Miyamachi Station|ja|宮町駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamachi Station|qq=}}. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Kotsu|qq=}} {{ill|Bus|ja|路線バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bus|qq=}}: 3-minute walk (300m) from "Miyamachi" {{ill|bus stop|ja|バス停留所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bus stop|qq=}}. ; By Car * {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Expressway|qq=}}: About 8 minutes (3.35 km) north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Nishi IC|qq=}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|qq=}} (Mikimoto Road). == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史』伊勢市役所、昭和43rd year 3月31日、954p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 3月5日、1690p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0601.html 清野井庭神社（きよのいばじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|清野井庭神社|version=101095922|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] }} [[ja:清野井庭神社]] [[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]]

{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyonoiba Shrine|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kiyonoiba-jinja 02.jpg|270px]]
Shrine Hall | location = 1-8 Tokiwachō, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|42|N|136|41|48.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Kayanohime|ja|草野姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanohime|qq=}} | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor Rank), {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Before [[Enryaku era|Enryaku]] 23 (804 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[Kinen-sai]] (Prayer Festival), {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|lt=Tsukinami-sai|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (Monthly Festival), among others }} '''{{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyonoiba Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Kiyonoiba Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}} (the Outer Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks 9th among the auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): Volume 1, p. 12. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise City|qq=}} Tokiwa 1-chome, 1197 Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 329 (in the residential address system: Tokiwa 1-chome, 8) is where the shrine is located. There is a theory that the former shrine site was at the {{ill|Ise Takayanagi Shopping Street|ja|伊勢高柳商店街|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Takayanagi Shopping Street|qq=}} in Miyamachi 1-chome, Ise City . If this theory is correct, it would suggest a different position for {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Oma Kokuso Shrine|ja|大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oma Kokuso Shrine|qq=}} as described in "Ruiju Jingihongen" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 330. The "Ujiyamada City History" suggests that the original Oma Kokuso Shrine had a larger precinct and that Kiyonoiba Shrine was established on the current site based on the situation during its reconstruction Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 936-937. The shrine grounds are rectangular, with an area of 1,095 square meters Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 333. The {{ill|enshrined deity|ja|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|qq=}} is {{ill|Kayanohime|ja|カヤノヒメ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanohime|qq=}} (Kayanohime no Mikoto Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 52). The deity is associated with the {{ill|irrigation water|ja|用水路|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/irrigation water|qq=}} near the shrine, though another theory suggests it is a {{ill|spirit|ja|分霊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/spirit|qq=}} of the deity of shipbuilding Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 52-53. "Jinmyocho Kosho Saiko" states that the enshrined deity is the same as {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|ja|志等美神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitomi Shrine|qq=}}. The {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/torii|qq=}} faces east, and the {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|qq=}} is located immediately to the right after entering Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 334. Built in {{ill|Shimmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimmei-zukuri|qq=}} style with wooden planks, it is surrounded by a {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagaki|qq=}} . There is no offering box. == History == [[File:Kiyonoibajinja sengu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Scene from the reconstruction on October 11, 2017.]] The name "Kiyonoiba" refers to the {{ill|wilderness|ja|原野|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wilderness|qq=}} around the shrine , while "Iba" means {{ill|weir|ja|堰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/weir|qq=}} . Over different {{ill|eras|ja|時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/eras|qq=}} and {{ill|texts|ja|書物|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/texts|qq=}}, "Iba" has been read as "iba," "iniwa," or "ite" . The shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, meaning it predates 927. It also appears in the "Toyukemiya Gishikicho" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 331, indicating its existence before 804. Rituals ceased by the late {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/medieval period|qq=}}, and the site was forgotten until {{ill|Oomiya-ji|ja|大宮司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oomiya-ji|qq=}} {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|qq=}} restored it on June 21, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: July 25, 1663) Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 329-332. During {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/World War II|qq=}}, the shrine was damaged in the {{ill|Ujiyamada Air Raid|ja|宇治山田空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Air Raid|qq=}} on July 29, 1945 Ise City (1968): p. 445. Postwar {{ill|land readjustment|ja|区画整理|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/land readjustment|qq=}} reduced the northern area and expanded the western part . The shrine was rebuilt on May 10, 1980 and again on October 11, 2017. == Rituals == {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinen-sai|qq=}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niinamesai|qq=}} (November) are conducted by a {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi|qq=}} at the shrine. {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} (January 3), {{ill|National Foundation Day|ja|建国記念祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Foundation Day|qq=}} (February 11), {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|qq=}} (February 23), and {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja|風日祈祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|qq=}} (May, August) are held remotely Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 332. == Flora == The precinct has tall trees such as {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enoki|qq=}} and {{ill|camphor trees|ja|クスノキ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/camphor trees|qq=}}, along with mid-sized trees like {{ill|arakashi|ja|アラカシ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/arakashi|qq=}} and {{ill|zelkova|ja|ケヤキ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zelkova|qq=}} . Newly added western areas have {{ill|coral trees|ja|サンゴジュ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/coral trees|qq=}} . == Access == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Central|qq=}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangu Line|qq=}}: About 300m southeast of {{ill|Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|ja|山田上口駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|qq=}} . * {{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu|qq=}} {{ill|Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Line|qq=}}: Around 5 minutes' walk (450m) from {{ill|Miyamachi Station|ja|宮町駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamachi Station|qq=}}. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Kotsu|qq=}} {{ill|Bus|ja|路線バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bus|qq=}}: 3-minute walk (300m) from "Miyamachi" {{ill|bus stop|ja|バス停留所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bus stop|qq=}}. ; By Car * {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Expressway|qq=}}: About 8 minutes (3.35 km) north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Nishi IC|qq=}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|qq=}} (Mikimoto Road). == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史』伊勢市役所、昭和43rd year 3月31日、954p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 3月5日、1690p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyonoiba Shrine|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kiyonoiba-jinja 02.jpg|270px]]
Shrine Hall | location = 1-8 Tokiwachō, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|42|N|136|41|48.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Kayanohime|ja|草野姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanohime|qq=}} | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor Rank), {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Before [[Enryaku era|Enryaku]] 23 (804 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[Kinensai|Kinen-sai]] (Prayer Festival), {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|lt=Tsukinami-sai|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (Monthly Festival), among others }} '''{{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyonoiba Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Kiyonoiba Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}} (the Outer Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks 9th among the auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): Volume 1, p. 12. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise City|qq=}} Tokiwa 1-chome, 1197 Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 329 (in the residential address system: Tokiwa 1-chome, 8) is where the shrine is located. There is a theory that the former shrine site was at the {{ill|Ise Takayanagi Shopping Street|ja|伊勢高柳商店街|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Takayanagi Shopping Street|qq=}} in Miyamachi 1-chome, Ise City . If this theory is correct, it would suggest a different position for {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Oma Kokuso Shrine|ja|大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oma Kokuso Shrine|qq=}} as described in "Ruiju Jingihongen" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 330. The "Ujiyamada City History" suggests that the original Oma Kokuso Shrine had a larger precinct and that Kiyonoiba Shrine was established on the current site based on the situation during its reconstruction Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 936-937. The shrine grounds are rectangular, with an area of 1,095 square meters Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 333. The {{ill|enshrined deity|ja|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|qq=}} is {{ill|Kayanohime|ja|カヤノヒメ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanohime|qq=}} (Kayanohime no Mikoto Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 52). The deity is associated with the {{ill|irrigation water|ja|用水路|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/irrigation water|qq=}} near the shrine, though another theory suggests it is a {{ill|spirit|ja|分霊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/spirit|qq=}} of the deity of shipbuilding Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 52-53. "Jinmyocho Kosho Saiko" states that the enshrined deity is the same as {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|ja|志等美神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitomi Shrine|qq=}}. The {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/torii|qq=}} faces east, and the {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|qq=}} is located immediately to the right after entering Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 334. Built in {{ill|Shimmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimmei-zukuri|qq=}} style with wooden planks, it is surrounded by a {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagaki|qq=}} . There is no offering box. == History == [[File:Kiyonoibajinja sengu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Scene from the reconstruction on October 11, 2017.]] The name "Kiyonoiba" refers to the {{ill|wilderness|ja|原野|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wilderness|qq=}} around the shrine , while "Iba" means {{ill|weir|ja|堰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/weir|qq=}} . Over different {{ill|eras|ja|時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/eras|qq=}} and {{ill|texts|ja|書物|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/texts|qq=}}, "Iba" has been read as "iba," "iniwa," or "ite" . The shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, meaning it predates 927. It also appears in the "Toyukemiya Gishikicho" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 331, indicating its existence before 804. Rituals ceased by the late {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/medieval period|qq=}}, and the site was forgotten until {{ill|Oomiya-ji|ja|大宮司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oomiya-ji|qq=}} {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|qq=}} restored it on June 21, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: July 25, 1663) Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 329-332. During {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/World War II|qq=}}, the shrine was damaged in the {{ill|Ujiyamada Air Raid|ja|宇治山田空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Air Raid|qq=}} on July 29, 1945 Ise City (1968): p. 445. Postwar {{ill|land readjustment|ja|区画整理|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/land readjustment|qq=}} reduced the northern area and expanded the western part . The shrine was rebuilt on May 10, 1980 and again on October 11, 2017. == Rituals == {{ill|Kinensai|lt=Kinensai|lt=Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinen-sai|qq=}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niinamesai|qq=}} (November) are conducted by a {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi|qq=}} at the shrine. {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} (January 3), {{ill|National Foundation Day|ja|建国記念祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Foundation Day|qq=}} (February 11), {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|qq=}} (February 23), and {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja|風日祈祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|qq=}} (May, August) are held remotely Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 332. == Flora == The precinct has tall trees such as {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enoki|qq=}} and {{ill|camphor trees|ja|クスノキ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/camphor trees|qq=}}, along with mid-sized trees like {{ill|arakashi|ja|アラカシ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/arakashi|qq=}} and {{ill|zelkova|ja|ケヤキ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zelkova|qq=}} . Newly added western areas have {{ill|coral trees|ja|サンゴジュ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/coral trees|qq=}} . == Access == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Central|qq=}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangu Line|qq=}}: About 300m southeast of {{ill|Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|ja|山田上口駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|qq=}} . * {{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu|qq=}} {{ill|Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Line|qq=}}: Around 5 minutes' walk (450m) from {{ill|Miyamachi Station|ja|宮町駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamachi Station|qq=}}. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Kotsu|qq=}} {{ill|Bus|ja|路線バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bus|qq=}}: 3-minute walk (300m) from "Miyamachi" {{ill|bus stop|ja|バス停留所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bus stop|qq=}}. ; By Car * {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Expressway|qq=}}: About 8 minutes (3.35 km) north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Nishi IC|qq=}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|qq=}} (Mikimoto Road). == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史』伊勢市役所、昭和43rd year 3月31日、954p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 3月5日、1690p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0601.html 清野井庭神社（きよのいばじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|清野井庭神社|version=101095922|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] }} [[ja:清野井庭神社]] [[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]]

revision.comment: Bot: fix redirect [[Niinamesai]] → [[Niiname-no-Matsuri]]

Postwar {{ill|land readjustment|ja|区画整理|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/land readjustment|qq=}} reduced the northern area and expanded the western part . The shrine was rebuilt on May 10, 1980 and again on October 11, 2017. == Rituals == {{ill|Kinensai|lt=Kinensai|lt=Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinen-sai|qq=}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}} (October), and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|lt=Niiname-no-Matsuri|lt=Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niinamesai|qq=}} (November) are conducted by a {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi|qq=}} at the shrine. {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} (January 3), {{ill|National Foundation Day|ja|建国記念祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Foundation Day|qq=}} (February 11), {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|qq=}} (February 23), and {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja|風日祈祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|qq=}} (May, August) are held remotely Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 332. == Flora == The precinct has tall trees such as {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enoki|qq=}} and {{ill|camphor trees|ja|クスノキ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/camphor trees|qq=}}, along with mid-sized trees like {{ill|arakashi|ja|アラカシ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/arakashi|qq=}} and {{ill|zelkova|ja|ケヤキ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zelkova|qq=}} . Newly added western areas have {{ill|coral trees|ja|サンゴジュ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/coral trees|qq=}} . == Access == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Central|qq=}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangu Line|qq=}}: About 300m southeast of {{ill|Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|ja|山田上口駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|qq=}} . * {{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu|qq=}} {{ill|Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Line|qq=}}: Around 5 minutes' walk (450m) from {{ill|Miyamachi Station|ja|宮町駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamachi Station|qq=}}. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Kotsu|qq=}} {{ill|Bus|ja|路線バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bus|qq=}}: 3-minute walk (300m) from "Miyamachi" {{ill|bus stop|ja|バス停留所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bus stop|qq=}}. ; By Car * {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Expressway|qq=}}: About 8 minutes (3.35 km) north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Nishi IC|qq=}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|qq=}} (Mikimoto Road). == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史』伊勢市役所、昭和43rd year 3月31日、954p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 3月5日、1690p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0601.html 清野井庭神社（きよのいばじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|清野井庭神社|version=101095922|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] }} [[ja:清野井庭神社]] [[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]]

revision.comment: Bot: fix redirect [[Enshrined deity]] → [[Saijin]]
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyonoiba Shrine|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kiyonoiba-jinja 02.jpg|270px]]
Shrine Hall | location = 1-8 Tokiwachō, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|42|N|136|41|48.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Kayanohime|ja|草野姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanohime|qq=}} | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor Rank), {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Before [[Enryaku era|Enryaku]] 23 (804 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[Kinensai|Kinen-sai]] (Prayer Festival), {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|lt=Tsukinami-sai|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (Monthly Festival), among others }} '''{{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyonoiba Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Kiyonoiba Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}} (the Outer Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks 9th among the auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): Volume 1, p. 12. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise City|qq=}} Tokiwa 1-chome, 1197 Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 329 (in the residential address system: Tokiwa 1-chome, 8) is where the shrine is located. There is a theory that the former shrine site was at the {{ill|Ise Takayanagi Shopping Street|ja|伊勢高柳商店街|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Takayanagi Shopping Street|qq=}} in Miyamachi 1-chome, Ise City . If this theory is correct, it would suggest a different position for {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Oma Kokuso Shrine|ja|大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oma Kokuso Shrine|qq=}} as described in "Ruiju Jingihongen" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 330. The "Ujiyamada City History" suggests that the original Oma Kokuso Shrine had a larger precinct and that Kiyonoiba Shrine was established on the current site based on the situation during its reconstruction Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 936-937. The shrine grounds are rectangular, with an area of 1,095 square meters Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 333. The {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deity|ja|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|qq=}} is {{ill|Kayanohime|ja|カヤノヒメ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanohime|qq=}} (Kayanohime no Mikoto Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 52). The deity is associated with the {{ill|irrigation water|ja|用水路|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/irrigation water|qq=}} near the shrine, though another theory suggests it is a {{ill|spirit|ja|分霊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/spirit|qq=}} of the deity of shipbuilding Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 52-53. "Jinmyocho Kosho Saiko" states that the enshrined deity is the same as {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|ja|志等美神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitomi Shrine|qq=}}. The {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/torii|qq=}} faces east, and the {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|qq=}} is located immediately to the right after entering Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 334. Built in {{ill|Shimmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimmei-zukuri|qq=}} style with wooden planks, it is surrounded by a {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagaki|qq=}} . There is no offering box. == History == [[File:Kiyonoibajinja sengu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Scene from the reconstruction on October 11, 2017.]] The name "Kiyonoiba" refers to the {{ill|wilderness|ja|原野|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wilderness|qq=}} around the shrine , while "Iba" means {{ill|weir|ja|堰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/weir|qq=}} . Over different {{ill|eras|ja|時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/eras|qq=}} and {{ill|texts|ja|書物|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/texts|qq=}}, "Iba" has been read as "iba," "iniwa," or "ite" . The shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, meaning it predates 927. It also appears in the "Toyukemiya Gishikicho" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 331, indicating its existence before 804. Rituals ceased by the late {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/medieval period|qq=}}, and the site was forgotten until {{ill|Oomiya-ji|ja|大宮司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oomiya-ji|qq=}} {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|qq=}} restored it on June 21, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: July 25, 1663) Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 329-332. During {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/World War II|qq=}}, the shrine was damaged in the {{ill|Ujiyamada Air Raid|ja|宇治山田空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Air Raid|qq=}} on July 29, 1945 Ise City (1968): p. 445. Postwar {{ill|land readjustment|ja|区画整理|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/land readjustment|qq=}} reduced the northern area and expanded the western part . The shrine was rebuilt on May 10, 1980 and again on October 11, 2017. == Rituals == {{ill|Kinensai|lt=Kinensai|lt=Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinen-sai|qq=}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}} (October), and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|lt=Niiname-no-Matsuri|lt=Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niinamesai|qq=}} (November) are conducted by a {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi|qq=}} at the shrine. {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} (January 3), {{ill|National Foundation Day|ja|建国記念祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Foundation Day|qq=}} (February 11), {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|qq=}} (February 23), and {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja|風日祈祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|qq=}} (May, August) are held remotely Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 332. == Flora == The precinct has tall trees such as {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enoki|qq=}} and {{ill|camphor trees|ja|クスノキ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/camphor trees|qq=}}, along with mid-sized trees like {{ill|arakashi|ja|アラカシ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/arakashi|qq=}} and {{ill|zelkova|ja|ケヤキ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zelkova|qq=}} . Newly added western areas have {{ill|coral trees|ja|サンゴジュ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/coral trees|qq=}} . == Access == ; Public Transportation * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Central|qq=}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangu Line|qq=}}: About 300m southeast of {{ill|Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|ja|山田上口駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|qq=}} . * {{ill|Kintetsu|ja|近畿日本鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu|qq=}} {{ill|Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Line|qq=}}: Around 5 minutes' walk (450m) from {{ill|Miyamachi Station|ja|宮町駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamachi Station|qq=}}. * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Kotsu|qq=}} {{ill|Bus|ja|路線バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bus|qq=}}: 3-minute walk (300m) from "Miyamachi" {{ill|bus stop|ja|バス停留所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bus stop|qq=}}. ; By Car * {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Expressway|qq=}}: About 8 minutes (3.35 km) north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Nishi IC|qq=}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|qq=}} (Mikimoto Road). == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史』伊勢市役所、昭和43rd year 3月31日、954p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 3月5日、1690p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0601.html 清野井庭神社（きよのいばじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|清野井庭神社|version=101095922|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] }} [[ja:清野井庭神社]] [[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]]

"Jinmyocho Kosho Saiko" states that the enshrined deity is the same as {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|ja|志等美神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitomi Shrine|qq=}}. The [[:en:Torii|torii]] faces east, and the {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|qq=}} is located immediately to the right after entering Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 334. Built in [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei-zukuri]] style with wooden planks, it is surrounded by a [[:en:Tamagaki|Tamagaki]] . There is no offering box. == History == [[File:Kiyonoibajinja sengu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Scene from the reconstruction on October 11, 2017.]] The name "Kiyonoiba" refers to the {{ill|wilderness|ja|原野|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wilderness|qq=}} around the shrine , while "Iba" means [[:en:Weir|weir]] . Over different {{ill|eras|ja|時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/eras|qq=}} and {{ill|texts|ja|書物|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/texts|qq=}}, "Iba" has been read as "iba," "iniwa," or "ite" . The shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, meaning it predates 927. It also appears in the "Toyukemiya Gishikicho" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 331, indicating its existence before 804. Rituals ceased by the late [[:en:Middle Ages|medieval period]], and the site was forgotten until {{ill|Oomiya-ji|ja|大宮司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oomiya-ji|qq=}} {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|qq=}} restored it on June 21, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: July 25, 1663) Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 329-332. During [[:en:World War II|World War II]], the shrine was damaged in the {{ill|Ujiyamada Air Raid|ja|宇治山田空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Air Raid|qq=}} on July 29, 1945 Ise City (1968): p. 445. Postwar {{ill|land readjustment|ja|区画整理|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/land readjustment|qq=}} reduced the northern area and expanded the western part . The shrine was rebuilt on May 10, 1980 and again on October 11, 2017. == Rituals == [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinen-sai]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June, December), [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October), and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] (November) are conducted by a {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi|qq=}} at the shrine. {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} (January 3), {{ill|National Foundation Day|ja|建国記念祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Foundation Day|qq=}} (February 11), {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|qq=}} (February 23), and {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja|風日祈祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|qq=}} (May, August) are held remotely Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 332. == Flora == The precinct has tall trees such as [[:en:Celtis sinensis|enoki]] and [[:en:Camphora officinarum|camphor trees]], along with mid-sized trees like [[:en:Quercus glauca|arakashi]] and [[:en:Zelkova serrata|zelkova]] . Newly added western areas have [[:en:Viburnum odoratissimum|coral trees]] . == Access == ; Public Transportation * [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]]: About 300m southeast of [[:en:Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|Yamada-Kamiguchi Station]] . * [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Yamada Line]]: Around 5 minutes' walk (450m) from [[:en:Miyamachi Station|Miyamachi Station]]. * [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] [[:en:Transit bus|Bus]]: 3-minute walk (300m) from "Miyamachi" [[:en:Bus stop|bus stop]]. ; By Car * [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]]: About 8 minutes (3.35 km) north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Nishi IC|qq=}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|qq=}} (Mikimoto Road). == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史』伊勢市役所、昭和43rd year 3月31日、954p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 3月5日、1690p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0601.html 清野井庭神社（きよのいばじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|清野井庭神社|version=101095922|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] }} [[ja:清野井庭神社]] [[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]]

{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyonoiba Shrine|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kiyonoiba-jinja 02.jpg|270px]]
Shrine Hall | location = 1-8 Tokiwachō, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|42|N|136|41|48.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Kayanohime|ja|草野姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanohime|qq=}} | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor Rank), {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Before [[Enryaku era|Enryaku]] 23 (804 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[Kinensai|Kinen-sai]] (Prayer Festival), {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|lt=Tsukinami-sai|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (Monthly Festival), among others }} '''{{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyonoiba Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Kiyonoiba Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}} (the Outer Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks 9th among the auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): Volume 1, p. 12. == Overview == [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]] Tokiwa 1-chome, 1197 Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 329 (in the residential address system: Tokiwa 1-chome, 8) is where the shrine is located. There is a theory that the former shrine site was at the {{ill|Ise Takayanagi Shopping Street|ja|伊勢高柳商店街|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Takayanagi Shopping Street|qq=}} in Miyamachi 1-chome, Ise City . If this theory is correct, it would suggest a different position for {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Oma Kokuso Shrine|ja|大間国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oma Kokuso Shrine|qq=}} as described in "Ruiju Jingihongen" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 330. The "Ujiyamada City History" suggests that the original Oma Kokuso Shrine had a larger precinct and that Kiyonoiba Shrine was established on the current site based on the situation during its reconstruction Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 936-937. The shrine grounds are rectangular, with an area of 1,095 square meters Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 333. The {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deity|ja|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|qq=}} is [[:en:Kaya-no-hime|Kayanohime]] (Kayanohime no Mikoto Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 52). The deity is associated with the {{ill|irrigation water|ja|用水路|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/irrigation water|qq=}} near the shrine, though another theory suggests it is a [[:en:Bunrei|spirit]] of the deity of shipbuilding Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 52-53. "Jinmyocho Kosho Saiko" states that the enshrined deity is the same as {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|ja|志等美神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitomi Shrine|qq=}}. The [[:en:Torii|torii]] faces east, and the {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|qq=}} is located immediately to the right after entering Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 334. Built in [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei-zukuri]] style with wooden planks, it is surrounded by a [[:en:Tamagaki|Tamagaki]] . There is no offering box. == History == [[File:Kiyonoibajinja sengu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Scene from the reconstruction on October 11, 2017.]] The name "Kiyonoiba" refers to the {{ill|wilderness|ja|原野|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wilderness|qq=}} around the shrine , while "Iba" means [[:en:Weir|weir]] . Over different {{ill|eras|ja|時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/eras|qq=}} and {{ill|texts|ja|書物|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/texts|qq=}}, "Iba" has been read as "iba," "iniwa," or "ite" . The shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, meaning it predates 927. It also appears in the "Toyukemiya Gishikicho" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 331, indicating its existence before 804. Rituals ceased by the late [[:en:Middle Ages|medieval period]], and the site was forgotten until {{ill|Oomiya-ji|ja|大宮司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oomiya-ji|qq=}} {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|qq=}} restored it on June 21, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: July 25, 1663) Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 329-332. During [[:en:World War II|World War II]], the shrine was damaged in the {{ill|Ujiyamada Air Raid|ja|宇治山田空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Air Raid|qq=}} on July 29, 1945 Ise City (1968): p. 445. Postwar {{ill|land readjustment|ja|区画整理|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/land readjustment|qq=}} reduced the northern area and expanded the western part . The shrine was rebuilt on May 10, 1980 and again on October 11, 2017. == Rituals == [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinen-sai]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June, December), [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October), and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] (November) are conducted by a {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi|qq=}} at the shrine. {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} (January 3), {{ill|National Foundation Day|ja|建国記念祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Foundation Day|qq=}} (February 11), {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|qq=}} (February 23), and {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja|風日祈祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|qq=}} (May, August) are held remotely Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 332. == Flora == The precinct has tall trees such as [[:en:Celtis sinensis|enoki]] and [[:en:Camphora officinarum|camphor trees]], along with mid-sized trees like [[:en:Quercus glauca|arakashi]] and [[:en:Zelkova serrata|zelkova]] . Newly added western areas have [[:en:Viburnum odoratissimum|coral trees]] . == Access == ; Public Transportation * [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]]: About 300m southeast of [[:en:Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|Yamada-Kamiguchi Station]] . * [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Yamada Line]]: Around 5 minutes' walk (450m) from [[:en:Miyamachi Station|Miyamachi Station]]. * [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] [[:en:Transit bus|Bus]]: 3-minute walk (300m) from "Miyamachi" [[:en:Bus stop|bus stop]]. ; By Car * [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]]: About 8 minutes (3.35 km) north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Nishi IC|qq=}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|qq=}} (Mikimoto Road). == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史』伊勢市役所、昭和43rd year 3月31日、954p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 3月5日、1690p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0601.html 清野井庭神社（きよのいばじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|清野井庭神社|version=101095922|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] }} [[ja:清野井庭神社]] [[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]]

Built in [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei-zukuri]] style with wooden planks, it is surrounded by a [[:en:Tamagaki|Tamagaki]] . There is no offering box. == History == [[File:Kiyonoibajinja sengu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Scene from the reconstruction on October 11, 2017.]] The name "Kiyonoiba" refers to the {{ill|wilderness|ja|原野|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wilderness|qq=}} around the shrine , while "Iba" means [[:en:Weir|weir]] . Over different {{ill|eras|ja|時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/eras|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and {{ill|texts|ja|書物|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/texts|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 本}}, "Iba" has been read as "iba," "iniwa," or "ite" . The shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, meaning it predates 927. It also appears in the "Toyukemiya Gishikicho" Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 331, indicating its existence before 804. Rituals ceased by the late [[:en:Middle Ages|medieval period]], and the site was forgotten until {{ill|Oomiya-ji|ja|大宮司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oomiya-ji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮司}} {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|qq=}} restored it on June 21, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: July 25, 1663) Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 329-332. During [[:en:World War II|World War II]], the shrine was damaged in the {{ill|Ujiyamada Air Raid|ja|宇治山田空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Air Raid|qq=}} on July 29, 1945 Ise City (1968): p. 445. Postwar {{ill|land readjustment|ja|区画整理|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/land readjustment|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 土地区画整理事業}} reduced the northern area and expanded the western part . The shrine was rebuilt on May 10, 1980 and again on October 11, 2017. == Rituals == [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinen-sai]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June, December), [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October), and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] (November) are conducted by a {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi|qq=}} at the shrine. {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} (January 3), {{ill|National Foundation Day|ja|建国記念祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Foundation Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (February 11), {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天皇誕生日}} (February 23), and {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja|風日祈祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (May, August) are held remotely Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 332. == Flora == The precinct has tall trees such as [[:en:Celtis sinensis|enoki]] and [[:en:Camphora officinarum|camphor trees]], along with mid-sized trees like [[:en:Quercus glauca|arakashi]] and [[:en:Zelkova serrata|zelkova]] . Newly added western areas have [[:en:Viburnum odoratissimum|coral trees]] . == Access == ; Public Transportation * [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]]: About 300m southeast of [[:en:Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|Yamada-Kamiguchi Station]] . * [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Yamada Line]]: Around 5 minutes' walk (450m) from [[:en:Miyamachi Station|Miyamachi Station]]. * [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] [[:en:Transit bus|Bus]]: 3-minute walk (300m) from "Miyamachi" [[:en:Bus stop|bus stop]]. ; By Car * [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]]: About 8 minutes (3.35 km) north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Nishi IC|qq=}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|qq=}} (Mikimoto Road). == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史』伊勢市役所、昭和43rd year 3月31日、954p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 3月5日、1690p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0601.html 清野井庭神社（きよのいばじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|清野井庭神社|version=101095922|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] }} [[ja:清野井庭神社]] [[sn:Kiyonoiba Shrine]]
