<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:清野井庭神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17213398]] Wikidata name: Kiyonoiba Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17213398]] Wikidata name: Kiyonoiba Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyonoiba Shrine|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Kiyonoiba-jinja 02.jpg | location = 1-8 Tokiwachō, [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34.495|N|136.696722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Kaya-no-hime|WD=Q8191878}} | type = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor Rank), {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Before [[Enryaku era|Enryaku]] 23 (804 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[Kinensai|Kinen-sai]] (Prayer Festival), {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|lt=Tsukinami-sai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (Monthly Festival), among others | location = {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }}
'''{{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyonoiba Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Kiyonoiba Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Outer Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks 9th among the auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): Volume 1, p. 12.</ref>
== Overview == [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]] Tokiwa 1-chome, 1197 <ref name="sk9">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 329</ref> (in the residential address system: Tokiwa 1-chome, 8) is where the shrine is located. There is a theory that the former shrine site was at the {{ill|Ise Takayanagi Shopping Street|ja|伊勢高柳商店街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Takayanagi Shopping Street|1=Ise Takayanagi Shopping Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11379159}} in Miyamachi 1-chome, Ise City <ref name="sk9"/>. If this theory is correct, it would suggest a different position for {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine|1=Kusanagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228323}} and {{ill|Oma Kokuso Shrine|ja|大間国生神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oma Kokuso Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 草奈伎神社|1=Kusanagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228323}} as described in "Ruiju Jingihongen" <ref name="sk0">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 330</ref>. The "Ujiyamada City History" suggests that the original Oma Kokuso Shrine had a larger precinct and that Kiyonoiba Shrine was established on the current site based on the situation during its reconstruction <ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 936-937</ref>. The shrine grounds are rectangular, with an area of 1,095 square meters <ref name="sk3">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 333</ref>.
The {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deity|ja|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミ}} is [[:en:Kaya-no-hime|Kayanohime]] <ref name="uy"/> (Kayanohime no Mikoto <ref name="ic2">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 52</ref>). The deity is associated with the {{ill|irrigation water|ja|用水路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/irrigation water|1=irrigation canal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2935978}} near the shrine, though another theory suggests it is a [[:en:Bunrei|spirit]] of the deity of shipbuilding <ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 52-53</ref>. "Jinmyocho Kosho Saiko" states that the enshrined deity is the same as {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|ja|志等美神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitomi Shrine|qq=}}.
The [[:en:Torii|torii]] faces east, and the {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is located immediately to the right after entering <ref name="sk4">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 334</ref>. Built in [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei-zukuri]] style with wooden planks, it is surrounded by a [[:en:Tamagaki|Tamagaki]] <ref name="sk4"/>. There is no offering box.
== History == [[File:Kiyonoibajinja sengu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Scene from the reconstruction on October 11, 2017.]]
The name "Kiyonoiba" refers to the {{ill|wilderness|ja|原野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wilderness|1=wilderness|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410074}} around the shrine <ref name="ic2"/>, while "Iba" means [[:en:Weir|weir]] <ref name="sk9"/>. Over different {{ill|eras|ja|時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/eras|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historical period|WD=Q11514315}} and {{ill|texts|ja|書物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/texts|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 本|1=book|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q571}}, "Iba" has been read as "iba," "iniwa," or "ite" <ref name="sk9"/>.
The shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, meaning it predates 927. It also appears in the "Toyukemiya Gishikicho" <ref name="sk1">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 331</ref>, indicating its existence before 804. Rituals ceased by the late [[:en:Middle Ages|medieval period]], and the site was forgotten until {{ill|Oomiya-ji|ja|大宮司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oomiya-ji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮司|1=Gūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453500}} {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|1=Kiyonaga Kawabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92924151}} restored it on June 21, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: July 25, 1663) <ref>Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 329-332</ref>.
During [[:en:World War II|World War II]], the shrine was damaged in the {{ill|Ujiyamada Air Raid|ja|宇治山田空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Air Raid|qq=}} on July 29, 1945 <ref>Ise City (1968): p. 445</ref>. Postwar {{ill|land readjustment|ja|区画整理|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/land readjustment|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 土地区画整理事業|1=land readjustment|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423116}} reduced the northern area and expanded the western part <ref name="sk3"/>.
The shrine was rebuilt on May 10, 1980 <ref name="sk4"/> and again on October 11, 2017.
== Rituals == [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinen-sai]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June, December), [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October), and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] (November) are conducted by a {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594490}} at the shrine. {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} (January 3), {{ill|National Foundation Day|ja|建国記念祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Foundation Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (February 11), {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天皇誕生日|1=The Emperor's Birthday|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q72318}} (February 23), and {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja|風日祈祭|1=Kazahi-no-Misai|2=ja|3=風日祈祭|WD=Q135524493|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524493}} (May, August) are held remotely <ref>Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 332</ref>.
== Flora == The precinct has tall trees such as [[:en:Celtis sinensis|enoki]] and [[:en:Camphora officinarum|camphor trees]], along with mid-sized trees like [[:en:Quercus glauca|arakashi]] and [[:en:Zelkova serrata|zelkova]] <ref name="sk3"/>. Newly added western areas have [[:en:Viburnum odoratissimum|coral trees]] <ref name="sk3"/>.

; By Car * [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]]: About 8 minutes (3.35 km) north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Mikimoto Road). == References == {{Reflist}}

It Ranks 9th among the auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): Volume 1, p. 12.</ref>
== Overview == [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]] Tokiwa 1-chome, 1197 <ref name="sk9">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 329</ref> (in the residential address system: Tokiwa 1-chome, 8) is where the shrine is located. If this theory is correct, it would suggest a different position for {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine|1=Kusanagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228323}} and {{ill|Oma Kokuso Shrine|ja|大間国生神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oma Kokuso Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 草奈伎神社|1=Kusanagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228323}} as described in "Ruiju Jingihongen" <ref name="sk0">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 330</ref>. The "Ujiyamada City History" suggests that the original Oma Kokuso Shrine had a larger precinct and that Kiyonoiba Shrine was established on the current site based on the situation during its reconstruction <ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 936-937</ref>. The shrine grounds are rectangular, with an area of 1,095 square meters <ref name="sk3">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 333</ref>.
The {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deity|ja|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミ}} is [[:en:Kaya-no-hime|Kayanohime]] <ref name="uy"/> (Kayanohime no Mikoto <ref name="ic2">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 52</ref>). The deity is associated with the {{ill|irrigation water|ja|用水路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/irrigation water|1=irrigation canal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2935978}} near the shrine, though another theory suggests it is a [[:en:Bunrei|spirit]] of the deity of shipbuilding <ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 52-53</ref>. "Jinmyocho Kosho Saiko" states that the enshrined deity is the same as {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|ja|志等美神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitomi Shrine|qq=}}.
The [[:en:Torii|torii]] faces east, and the {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is located immediately to the right after entering <ref name="sk4">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 334</ref>. Built in [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei-zukuri]] style with wooden planks, it is surrounded by a [[:en:Tamagaki|Tamagaki]] <ref name="sk4"/>. There is no offering box.
== History == [[File:Kiyonoibajinja sengu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Scene from the reconstruction on October 11, 2017.]]
The name "Kiyonoiba" refers to the {{ill|wilderness|ja|原野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wilderness|1=wilderness|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410074}} around the shrine <ref name="ic2"/>, while "Iba" means [[:en:Weir|weir]] <ref name="sk9"/>. Over different {{ill|eras|ja|時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/eras|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historical period|WD=Q11514315}} and {{ill|texts|ja|書物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/texts|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 本|1=book|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q571}}, "Iba" has been read as "iba," "iniwa," or "ite" <ref name="sk9"/>.
The shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, meaning it predates 927. It also appears in the "Toyukemiya Gishikicho" <ref name="sk1">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 331</ref>, indicating its existence before 804. Rituals ceased by the late [[:en:Middle Ages|medieval period]], and the site was forgotten until {{ill|Oomiya-ji|ja|大宮司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oomiya-ji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮司|1=Gūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453500}} {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|1=Kiyonaga Kawabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92924151}} restored it on June 21, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: July 25, 1663) <ref>Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 329-332</ref>.
During [[:en:World War II|World War II]], the shrine was damaged in the {{ill|Ujiyamada Air Raid|ja|宇治山田空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Air Raid|qq=}} on July 29, 1945 <ref>Ise City (1968): p. 445</ref>. Postwar {{ill|land readjustment|ja|区画整理|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/land readjustment|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 土地区画整理事業|1=land readjustment|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423116}} reduced the northern area and expanded the western part <ref name="sk3"/>.
The shrine was rebuilt on May 10, 1980 <ref name="sk4"/> and again on October 11, 2017.
== Rituals == [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinen-sai]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June, December), [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October), and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] (November) are conducted by a {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594490}} at the shrine. {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} (January 3), {{ill|National Foundation Day|ja|建国記念祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Foundation Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (February 11), {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天皇誕生日|1=The Emperor's Birthday|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q72318}} (February 23), and {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja|風日祈祭|1=Kazahi-no-Misai|2=ja|3=風日祈祭|WD=Q135524493|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524493}} (May, August) are held remotely <ref>Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 332</ref>.
== Flora == The precinct has tall trees such as [[:en:Celtis sinensis|enoki]] and [[:en:Camphora officinarum|camphor trees]], along with mid-sized trees like [[:en:Quercus glauca|arakashi]] and [[:en:Zelkova serrata|zelkova]] <ref name="sk3"/>. Newly added western areas have [[:en:Viburnum odoratissimum|coral trees]] <ref name="sk3"/>.
== Access == ; Public Transportation * [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]]: About 300m southeast of [[:en:Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|Yamada-Kamiguchi Station]] <ref name="sk9"/>. * [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Yamada Line]]: Around 5 minutes' walk (450m) from [[:en:Miyamachi Station|Miyamachi Station]]. * [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] [[:en:Transit bus|Bus]]: 3-minute walk (300m) from "Miyamachi" [[:en:Bus stop|bus stop]].
; By Car * [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]]: About 8 minutes (3.35 km) north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Mikimoto Road). == References == {{Reflist}}

ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:清野井庭神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17213398]] Wikidata name: Kiyonoiba Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17213398]] Wikidata name: Kiyonoiba Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyonoiba Shrine|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Kiyonoiba-jinja 02.jpg | location = 1-8 Tokiwachō, [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34.495|N|136.696722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Kaya-no-hime|WD=Q8191878}} | type = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor Rank), {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Before [[Enryaku era|Enryaku]] 23 (804 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[Kinensai|Kinen-sai]] (Prayer Festival), {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|lt=Tsukinami-sai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (Monthly Festival), among others | location = {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }}
'''{{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyonoiba Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Kiyonoiba Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Outer Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks 9th among the auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): Volume 1, p. 12.</ref>
== Overview == [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]] Tokiwa 1-chome, 1197 <ref name="sk9">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 329</ref> (in the residential address system: Tokiwa 1-chome, 8) is where the shrine is located. There is a theory that the former shrine site was at the {{ill|Ise Takayanagi Shopping Street|ja|伊勢高柳商店街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Takayanagi Shopping Street|1=Ise Takayanagi Shopping Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11379159}} in Miyamachi 1-chome, Ise City <ref name="sk9"/>. If this theory is correct, it would suggest a different position for {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine|1=Kusanagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228323}} and {{ill|Oma Kokuso Shrine|ja|大間国生神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oma Kokuso Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 草奈伎神社|1=Kusanagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228323}} as described in "Ruiju Jingihongen" <ref name="sk0">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 330</ref>. The "Ujiyamada City History" suggests that the original Oma Kokuso Shrine had a larger precinct and that Kiyonoiba Shrine was established on the current site based on the situation during its reconstruction <ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 936-937</ref>. The shrine grounds are rectangular, with an area of 1,095 square meters <ref name="sk3">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 333</ref>.
The {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deity|ja|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミ}} is [[:en:Kaya-no-hime|Kayanohime]] <ref name="uy"/> (Kayanohime no Mikoto <ref name="ic2">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 52</ref>). The deity is associated with the {{ill|irrigation water|ja|用水路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/irrigation water|1=irrigation canal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2935978}} near the shrine, though another theory suggests it is a [[:en:Bunrei|spirit]] of the deity of shipbuilding <ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 52-53</ref>. "Jinmyocho Kosho Saiko" states that the enshrined deity is the same as {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|ja|志等美神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitomi Shrine|qq=}}.
The [[:en:Torii|torii]] faces east, and the {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is located immediately to the right after entering <ref name="sk4">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 334</ref>. Built in [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei-zukuri]] style with wooden planks, it is surrounded by a [[:en:Tamagaki|Tamagaki]] <ref name="sk4"/>. There is no offering box.
== History == [[File:Kiyonoibajinja sengu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Scene from the reconstruction on October 11, 2017.]]
The name "Kiyonoiba" refers to the {{ill|wilderness|ja|原野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wilderness|1=wilderness|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410074}} around the shrine <ref name="ic2"/>, while "Iba" means [[:en:Weir|weir]] <ref name="sk9"/>. Over different {{ill|eras|ja|時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/eras|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historical period|WD=Q11514315}} and {{ill|texts|ja|書物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/texts|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 本|1=book|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q571}}, "Iba" has been read as "iba," "iniwa," or "ite" <ref name="sk9"/>.
The shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, meaning it predates 927. It also appears in the "Toyukemiya Gishikicho" <ref name="sk1">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 331</ref>, indicating its existence before 804. Rituals ceased by the late [[:en:Middle Ages|medieval period]], and the site was forgotten until {{ill|Oomiya-ji|ja|大宮司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oomiya-ji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮司|1=Gūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453500}} {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|1=Kiyonaga Kawabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92924151}} restored it on June 21, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: July 25, 1663) <ref>Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 329-332</ref>.
During [[:en:World War II|World War II]], the shrine was damaged in the {{ill|Ujiyamada Air Raid|ja|宇治山田空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Air Raid|qq=}} on July 29, 1945 <ref>Ise City (1968): p. 445</ref>. Postwar {{ill|land readjustment|ja|区画整理|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/land readjustment|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 土地区画整理事業|1=land readjustment|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423116}} reduced the northern area and expanded the western part <ref name="sk3"/>.
The shrine was rebuilt on May 10, 1980 <ref name="sk4"/> and again on October 11, 2017.
== Rituals == [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinen-sai]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June, December), [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October), and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] (November) are conducted by a {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594490}} at the shrine. {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} (January 3), {{ill|National Foundation Day|ja|建国記念祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Foundation Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (February 11), {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天皇誕生日|1=The Emperor's Birthday|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q72318}} (February 23), and {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja|風日祈祭|1=Kazahi-no-Misai|2=ja|3=風日祈祭|WD=Q135524493|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524493}} (May, August) are held remotely <ref>Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 332</ref>.
== Flora == The precinct has tall trees such as [[:en:Celtis sinensis|enoki]] and [[:en:Camphora officinarum|camphor trees]], along with mid-sized trees like [[:en:Quercus glauca|arakashi]] and [[:en:Zelkova serrata|zelkova]] <ref name="sk3"/>. Newly added western areas have [[:en:Viburnum odoratissimum|coral trees]] <ref name="sk3"/>.
== Access == ; Public Transportation * [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]]: About 300m southeast of [[:en:Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|Yamada-Kamiguchi Station]] <ref name="sk9"/>. * [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Yamada Line]]: Around 5 minutes' walk (450m) from [[:en:Miyamachi Station|Miyamachi Station]]. * [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] [[:en:Transit bus|Bus]]: 3-minute walk (300m) from "Miyamachi" [[:en:Bus stop|bus stop]].
; By Car * [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]]: About 8 minutes (3.35 km) north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Mikimoto Road). == References == {{Reflist}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:清野井庭神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17213398]] Wikidata name: Kiyonoiba Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17213398]] Wikidata name: Kiyonoiba Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyonoiba Shrine|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Kiyonoiba-jinja 02.jpg | location = 1-8 Tokiwachō, [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34.495|N|136.696722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Kaya-no-hime|WD=Q8191878}} | type = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor Rank), {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Before [[Enryaku era|Enryaku]] 23 (804 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[Kinensai|Kinen-sai]] (Prayer Festival), {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|lt=Tsukinami-sai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (Monthly Festival), among others | location = {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }}
'''{{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyonoiba Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Kiyonoiba Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Outer Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks 9th among the auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): Volume 1, p. 12.</ref>
== Overview == [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]] Tokiwa 1-chome, 1197 <ref name="sk9">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 329</ref> (in the residential address system: Tokiwa 1-chome, 8) is where the shrine is located. There is a theory that the former shrine site was at the {{ill|Ise Takayanagi Shopping Street|ja|伊勢高柳商店街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Takayanagi Shopping Street|1=Ise Takayanagi Shopping Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11379159}} in Miyamachi 1-chome, Ise City <ref name="sk9"/>. If this theory is correct, it would suggest a different position for {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine|1=Kusanagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228323}} and {{ill|Oma Kokuso Shrine|ja|大間国生神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oma Kokuso Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 草奈伎神社|1=Kusanagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228323}} as described in "Ruiju Jingihongen" <ref name="sk0">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 330</ref>. The "Ujiyamada City History" suggests that the original Oma Kokuso Shrine had a larger precinct and that Kiyonoiba Shrine was established on the current site based on the situation during its reconstruction <ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 936-937</ref>. The shrine grounds are rectangular, with an area of 1,095 square meters <ref name="sk3">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 333</ref>.
The {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deity|ja|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミ}} is [[:en:Kaya-no-hime|Kayanohime]] <ref name="uy"/> (Kayanohime no Mikoto <ref name="ic2">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 52</ref>). The deity is associated with the {{ill|irrigation water|ja|用水路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/irrigation water|1=irrigation canal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2935978}} near the shrine, though another theory suggests it is a [[:en:Bunrei|spirit]] of the deity of shipbuilding <ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 52-53</ref>. "Jinmyocho Kosho Saiko" states that the enshrined deity is the same as {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|ja|志等美神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitomi Shrine|qq=}}.
The [[:en:Torii|torii]] faces east, and the {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is located immediately to the right after entering <ref name="sk4">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 334</ref>. Built in [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei-zukuri]] style with wooden planks, it is surrounded by a [[:en:Tamagaki|Tamagaki]] <ref name="sk4"/>. There is no offering box.
== History == [[File:Kiyonoibajinja sengu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Scene from the reconstruction on October 11, 2017.]]
The name "Kiyonoiba" refers to the {{ill|wilderness|ja|原野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wilderness|1=wilderness|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410074}} around the shrine <ref name="ic2"/>, while "Iba" means [[:en:Weir|weir]] <ref name="sk9"/>. Over different {{ill|eras|ja|時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/eras|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historical period|WD=Q11514315}} and {{ill|texts|ja|書物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/texts|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 本|1=book|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q571}}, "Iba" has been read as "iba," "iniwa," or "ite" <ref name="sk9"/>.
The shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, meaning it predates 927. It also appears in the "Toyukemiya Gishikicho" <ref name="sk1">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 331</ref>, indicating its existence before 804. Rituals ceased by the late [[:en:Middle Ages|medieval period]], and the site was forgotten until {{ill|Oomiya-ji|ja|大宮司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oomiya-ji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮司|1=Gūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453500}} {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|1=Kiyonaga Kawabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92924151}} restored it on June 21, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: July 25, 1663) <ref>Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 329-332</ref>.
During [[:en:World War II|World War II]], the shrine was damaged in the {{ill|Ujiyamada Air Raid|ja|宇治山田空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Air Raid|qq=}} on July 29, 1945 <ref>Ise City (1968): p. 445</ref>. Postwar {{ill|land readjustment|ja|区画整理|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/land readjustment|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 土地区画整理事業|1=land readjustment|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423116}} reduced the northern area and expanded the western part <ref name="sk3"/>.
The shrine was rebuilt on May 10, 1980 <ref name="sk4"/> and again on October 11, 2017.
== Rituals == [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinen-sai]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June, December), [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October), and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] (November) are conducted by a {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594490}} at the shrine. {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} (January 3), {{ill|National Foundation Day|ja|建国記念祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Foundation Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (February 11), {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天皇誕生日|1=The Emperor's Birthday|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q72318}} (February 23), and {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja|風日祈祭|1=Kazahi-no-Misai|2=ja|3=風日祈祭|WD=Q135524493|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524493}} (May, August) are held remotely <ref>Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 332</ref>.
== Flora == The precinct has tall trees such as [[:en:Celtis sinensis|enoki]] and [[:en:Camphora officinarum|camphor trees]], along with mid-sized trees like [[:en:Quercus glauca|arakashi]] and [[:en:Zelkova serrata|zelkova]] <ref name="sk3"/>. Newly added western areas have [[:en:Viburnum odoratissimum|coral trees]] <ref name="sk3"/>.
== Access == ; Public Transportation * [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]]: About 300m southeast of [[:en:Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|Yamada-Kamiguchi Station]] <ref name="sk9"/>. * [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Yamada Line]]: Around 5 minutes' walk (450m) from [[:en:Miyamachi Station|Miyamachi Station]]. * [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] [[:en:Transit bus|Bus]]: 3-minute walk (300m) from "Miyamachi" [[:en:Bus stop|bus stop]].
; By Car * [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]]: About 8 minutes (3.35 km) north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Mikimoto Road). == References == {{Reflist}}

"Jinmyocho Kosho Saiko" states that the enshrined deity is the same as {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|ja|志等美神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitomi Shrine|qq=}}.
The [[:en:Torii|torii]] faces east, and the {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is located immediately to the right after entering <ref name="sk4">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 334</ref>. == History == [[File:Kiyonoibajinja sengu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Scene from the reconstruction on October 11, 2017.]]
The name "Kiyonoiba" refers to the {{ill|wilderness|ja|原野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wilderness|1=wilderness|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410074}} around the shrine <ref name="ic2"/>, while "Iba" means [[:en:Weir|weir]] <ref name="sk9"/>. Over different {{ill|eras|ja|時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/eras|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historical period|WD=Q11514315}} and {{ill|texts|ja|書物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/texts|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 本|1=book|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q571}}, "Iba" has been read as "iba," "iniwa," or "ite" <ref name="sk9"/>.
The shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, meaning it predates 927. It also appears in the "Toyukemiya Gishikicho" <ref name="sk1">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 331</ref>, indicating its existence before 804. Rituals ceased by the late [[:en:Middle Ages|medieval period]], and the site was forgotten until {{ill|Oomiya-ji|ja|大宮司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oomiya-ji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮司|1=Gūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453500}} {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|1=Kiyonaga Kawabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92924151}} restored it on June 21, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: July 25, 1663) <ref>Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 329-332</ref>.
During [[:en:World War II|World War II]], the shrine was damaged in the {{ill|Ujiyamada Air Raid|ja|宇治山田空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Air Raid|qq=}} on July 29, 1945 <ref>Ise City (1968): p. 445</ref>. Postwar {{ill|land readjustment|ja|区画整理|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/land readjustment|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 土地区画整理事業|1=land readjustment|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423116}} reduced the northern area and expanded the western part <ref name="sk3"/>.
The shrine was rebuilt on May 10, 1980 <ref name="sk4"/> and again on October 11, 2017.
== Rituals == [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinen-sai]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June, December), [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October), and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] (November) are conducted by a {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594490}} at the shrine. {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} (January 3), {{ill|National Foundation Day|ja|建国記念祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Foundation Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (February 11), {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天皇誕生日|1=The Emperor's Birthday|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q72318}} (February 23), and {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja|風日祈祭|1=Kazahi-no-Misai|2=ja|3=風日祈祭|WD=Q135524493|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524493}} (May, August) are held remotely <ref>Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 332</ref>.
== Flora == The precinct has tall trees such as [[:en:Celtis sinensis|enoki]] and [[:en:Camphora officinarum|camphor trees]], along with mid-sized trees like [[:en:Quercus glauca|arakashi]] and [[:en:Zelkova serrata|zelkova]] <ref name="sk3"/>. Newly added western areas have [[:en:Viburnum odoratissimum|coral trees]] <ref name="sk3"/>.
== Access == ; Public Transportation * [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]]: About 300m southeast of [[:en:Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|Yamada-Kamiguchi Station]] <ref name="sk9"/>. * [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Yamada Line]]: Around 5 minutes' walk (450m) from [[:en:Miyamachi Station|Miyamachi Station]]. * [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] [[:en:Transit bus|Bus]]: 3-minute walk (300m) from "Miyamachi" [[:en:Bus stop|bus stop]].
; By Car * [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]]: About 8 minutes (3.35 km) north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Mikimoto Road). == References == {{Reflist}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:清野井庭神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17213398]] Wikidata name: Kiyonoiba Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17213398]] Wikidata name: Kiyonoiba Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyonoiba Shrine|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Kiyonoiba-jinja 02.jpg | location = 1-8 Tokiwachō, [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34.495|N|136.696722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Kaya-no-hime|WD=Q8191878}} | type = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor Rank), {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Before [[Enryaku era|Enryaku]] 23 (804 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[Kinensai|Kinen-sai]] (Prayer Festival), {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|lt=Tsukinami-sai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (Monthly Festival), among others | location = {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }}
'''{{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyonoiba Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Kiyonoiba Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Outer Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks 9th among the auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): Volume 1, p. 12.</ref>
== Overview == [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]] Tokiwa 1-chome, 1197 <ref name="sk9">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 329</ref> (in the residential address system: Tokiwa 1-chome, 8) is where the shrine is located. There is a theory that the former shrine site was at the {{ill|Ise Takayanagi Shopping Street|ja|伊勢高柳商店街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Takayanagi Shopping Street|1=Ise Takayanagi Shopping Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11379159}} in Miyamachi 1-chome, Ise City <ref name="sk9"/>. If this theory is correct, it would suggest a different position for {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine|1=Kusanagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228323}} and {{ill|Oma Kokuso Shrine|ja|大間国生神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oma Kokuso Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 草奈伎神社|1=Kusanagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228323}} as described in "Ruiju Jingihongen" <ref name="sk0">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 330</ref>. The "Ujiyamada City History" suggests that the original Oma Kokuso Shrine had a larger precinct and that Kiyonoiba Shrine was established on the current site based on the situation during its reconstruction <ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 936-937</ref>. The shrine grounds are rectangular, with an area of 1,095 square meters <ref name="sk3">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 333</ref>.
The {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deity|ja|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミ}} is [[:en:Kaya-no-hime|Kayanohime]] <ref name="uy"/> (Kayanohime no Mikoto <ref name="ic2">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 52</ref>). The deity is associated with the {{ill|irrigation water|ja|用水路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/irrigation water|1=irrigation canal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2935978}} near the shrine, though another theory suggests it is a [[:en:Bunrei|spirit]] of the deity of shipbuilding <ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 52-53</ref>. "Jinmyocho Kosho Saiko" states that the enshrined deity is the same as {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|ja|志等美神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitomi Shrine|qq=}}.
The [[:en:Torii|torii]] faces east, and the {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is located immediately to the right after entering <ref name="sk4">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 334</ref>. Built in [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei-zukuri]] style with wooden planks, it is surrounded by a [[:en:Tamagaki|Tamagaki]] <ref name="sk4"/>. There is no offering box.
== History == [[File:Kiyonoibajinja sengu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Scene from the reconstruction on October 11, 2017.]]
The name "Kiyonoiba" refers to the {{ill|wilderness|ja|原野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wilderness|1=wilderness|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410074}} around the shrine <ref name="ic2"/>, while "Iba" means [[:en:Weir|weir]] <ref name="sk9"/>. Over different {{ill|eras|ja|時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/eras|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historical period|WD=Q11514315}} and {{ill|texts|ja|書物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/texts|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 本|1=book|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q571}}, "Iba" has been read as "iba," "iniwa," or "ite" <ref name="sk9"/>.
The shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, meaning it predates 927. It also appears in the "Toyukemiya Gishikicho" <ref name="sk1">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 331</ref>, indicating its existence before 804. Rituals ceased by the late [[:en:Middle Ages|medieval period]], and the site was forgotten until {{ill|Oomiya-ji|ja|大宮司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oomiya-ji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮司|1=Gūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453500}} {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|1=Kiyonaga Kawabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92924151}} restored it on June 21, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: July 25, 1663) <ref>Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 329-332</ref>.
During [[:en:World War II|World War II]], the shrine was damaged in the {{ill|Ujiyamada Air Raid|ja|宇治山田空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Air Raid|qq=}} on July 29, 1945 <ref>Ise City (1968): p. 445</ref>. Postwar {{ill|land readjustment|ja|区画整理|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/land readjustment|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 土地区画整理事業|1=land readjustment|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423116}} reduced the northern area and expanded the western part <ref name="sk3"/>.
The shrine was rebuilt on May 10, 1980 <ref name="sk4"/> and again on October 11, 2017.
== Rituals == [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinen-sai]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June, December), [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October), and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] (November) are conducted by a {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594490}} at the shrine. {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} (January 3), {{ill|National Foundation Day|ja|建国記念祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Foundation Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (February 11), {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天皇誕生日|1=The Emperor's Birthday|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q72318}} (February 23), and {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja|風日祈祭|1=Kazahi-no-Misai|2=ja|3=風日祈祭|WD=Q135524493|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524493}} (May, August) are held remotely <ref>Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 332</ref>.
== Flora == The precinct has tall trees such as [[:en:Celtis sinensis|enoki]] and [[:en:Camphora officinarum|camphor trees]], along with mid-sized trees like [[:en:Quercus glauca|arakashi]] and [[:en:Zelkova serrata|zelkova]] <ref name="sk3"/>. Newly added western areas have [[:en:Viburnum odoratissimum|coral trees]] <ref name="sk3"/>.

; By Car * [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]]: About 8 minutes (3.35 km) north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Mikimoto Road). == References == {{Reflist}}

'''{{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyonoiba Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Kiyonoiba Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Outer Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks 9th among the auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): Volume 1, p. 12.</ref>
== Overview == [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]] Tokiwa 1-chome, 1197 <ref name="sk9">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 329</ref> (in the residential address system: Tokiwa 1-chome, 8) is where the shrine is located. There is a theory that the former shrine site was at the {{ill|Ise Takayanagi Shopping Street|ja|伊勢高柳商店街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Takayanagi Shopping Street|1=Ise Takayanagi Shopping Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11379159}} in Miyamachi 1-chome, Ise City <ref name="sk9"/>. If this theory is correct, it would suggest a different position for {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine|1=Kusanagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228323}} and {{ill|Oma Kokuso Shrine|ja|大間国生神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oma Kokuso Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 草奈伎神社|1=Kusanagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228323}} as described in "Ruiju Jingihongen" <ref name="sk0">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 330</ref>. The "Ujiyamada City History" suggests that the original Oma Kokuso Shrine had a larger precinct and that Kiyonoiba Shrine was established on the current site based on the situation during its reconstruction <ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 936-937</ref>. The shrine grounds are rectangular, with an area of 1,095 square meters <ref name="sk3">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 333</ref>.
The {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deity|ja|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミ}} is [[:en:Kaya-no-hime|Kayanohime]] <ref name="uy"/> (Kayanohime no Mikoto <ref name="ic2">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 52</ref>). The deity is associated with the {{ill|irrigation water|ja|用水路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/irrigation water|1=irrigation canal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2935978}} near the shrine, though another theory suggests it is a [[:en:Bunrei|spirit]] of the deity of shipbuilding <ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 52-53</ref>. "Jinmyocho Kosho Saiko" states that the enshrined deity is the same as {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|ja|志等美神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitomi Shrine|qq=}}.
The [[:en:Torii|torii]] faces east, and the {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is located immediately to the right after entering <ref name="sk4">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 334</ref>. Built in [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei-zukuri]] style with wooden planks, it is surrounded by a [[:en:Tamagaki|Tamagaki]] <ref name="sk4"/>. There is no offering box.
== History == [[File:Kiyonoibajinja sengu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Scene from the reconstruction on October 11, 2017.]]
The name "Kiyonoiba" refers to the {{ill|wilderness|ja|原野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wilderness|1=wilderness|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410074}} around the shrine <ref name="ic2"/>, while "Iba" means [[:en:Weir|weir]] <ref name="sk9"/>. Over different {{ill|eras|ja|時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/eras|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historical period|WD=Q11514315}} and {{ill|texts|ja|書物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/texts|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 本|1=book|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q571}}, "Iba" has been read as "iba," "iniwa," or "ite" <ref name="sk9"/>.
The shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, meaning it predates 927. It also appears in the "Toyukemiya Gishikicho" <ref name="sk1">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 331</ref>, indicating its existence before 804. Rituals ceased by the late [[:en:Middle Ages|medieval period]], and the site was forgotten until {{ill|Oomiya-ji|ja|大宮司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oomiya-ji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮司|1=Gūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453500}} {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|1=Kiyonaga Kawabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92924151}} restored it on June 21, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: July 25, 1663) <ref>Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 329-332</ref>.
During [[:en:World War II|World War II]], the shrine was damaged in the {{ill|Ujiyamada Air Raid|ja|宇治山田空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Air Raid|qq=}} on July 29, 1945 <ref>Ise City (1968): p. 445</ref>. Postwar {{ill|land readjustment|ja|区画整理|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/land readjustment|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 土地区画整理事業|1=land readjustment|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423116}} reduced the northern area and expanded the western part <ref name="sk3"/>.
The shrine was rebuilt on May 10, 1980 <ref name="sk4"/> and again on October 11, 2017.
== Rituals == [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinen-sai]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June, December), [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October), and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] (November) are conducted by a {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594490}} at the shrine. {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} (January 3), {{ill|National Foundation Day|ja|建国記念祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Foundation Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (February 11), {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天皇誕生日|1=The Emperor's Birthday|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q72318}} (February 23), and {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja|風日祈祭|1=Kazahi-no-Misai|2=ja|3=風日祈祭|WD=Q135524493|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524493}} (May, August) are held remotely <ref>Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 332</ref>.
== Flora == The precinct has tall trees such as [[:en:Celtis sinensis|enoki]] and [[:en:Camphora officinarum|camphor trees]], along with mid-sized trees like [[:en:Quercus glauca|arakashi]] and [[:en:Zelkova serrata|zelkova]] <ref name="sk3"/>. Newly added western areas have [[:en:Viburnum odoratissimum|coral trees]] <ref name="sk3"/>.
== Access == ; Public Transportation * [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]]: About 300m southeast of [[:en:Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|Yamada-Kamiguchi Station]] <ref name="sk9"/>. * [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Yamada Line]]: Around 5 minutes' walk (450m) from [[:en:Miyamachi Station|Miyamachi Station]]. * [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] [[:en:Transit bus|Bus]]: 3-minute walk (300m) from "Miyamachi" [[:en:Bus stop|bus stop]].
; By Car * [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]]: About 8 minutes (3.35 km) north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Mikimoto Road). == References == {{Reflist}}

'''{{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyonoiba Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Kiyonoiba Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Outer Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks 9th among the auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): Volume 1, p. 12.</ref>
== Overview == [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]] Tokiwa 1-chome, 1197 <ref name="sk9">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 329</ref> (in the residential address system: Tokiwa 1-chome, 8) is where the shrine is located. There is a theory that the former shrine site was at the {{ill|Ise Takayanagi Shopping Street|ja|伊勢高柳商店街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Takayanagi Shopping Street|1=Ise Takayanagi Shopping Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11379159}} in Miyamachi 1-chome, Ise City <ref name="sk9"/>. If this theory is correct, it would suggest a different position for {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine|1=Kusanagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228323}} and {{ill|Oma Kokuso Shrine|ja|大間国生神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oma Kokuso Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 草奈伎神社|1=Kusanagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228323}} as described in "Ruiju Jingihongen" <ref name="sk0">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 330</ref>. The "Ujiyamada City History" suggests that the original Oma Kokuso Shrine had a larger precinct and that Kiyonoiba Shrine was established on the current site based on the situation during its reconstruction <ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 936-937</ref>. The shrine grounds are rectangular, with an area of 1,095 square meters <ref name="sk3">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 333</ref>.
The {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deity|ja|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミ}} is [[:en:Kaya-no-hime|Kayanohime]] <ref name="uy"/> (Kayanohime no Mikoto <ref name="ic2">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 52</ref>). The deity is associated with the {{ill|irrigation water|ja|用水路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/irrigation water|1=irrigation canal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2935978}} near the shrine, though another theory suggests it is a [[:en:Bunrei|spirit]] of the deity of shipbuilding <ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 52-53</ref>. "Jinmyocho Kosho Saiko" states that the enshrined deity is the same as {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|ja|志等美神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitomi Shrine|qq=}}.
The [[:en:Torii|torii]] faces east, and the {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is located immediately to the right after entering <ref name="sk4">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 334</ref>. Built in [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei-zukuri]] style with wooden planks, it is surrounded by a [[:en:Tamagaki|Tamagaki]] <ref name="sk4"/>. There is no offering box.
== History == [[File:Kiyonoibajinja sengu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Scene from the reconstruction on October 11, 2017.]]
The name "Kiyonoiba" refers to the {{ill|wilderness|ja|原野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wilderness|1=wilderness|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410074}} around the shrine <ref name="ic2"/>, while "Iba" means [[:en:Weir|weir]] <ref name="sk9"/>. Over different {{ill|eras|ja|時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/eras|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historical period|WD=Q11514315}} and {{ill|texts|ja|書物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/texts|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 本|1=book|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q571}}, "Iba" has been read as "iba," "iniwa," or "ite" <ref name="sk9"/>.

It also appears in the "Toyukemiya Gishikicho" <ref name="sk1">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 331</ref>, indicating its existence before 804. Postwar {{ill|land readjustment|ja|区画整理|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/land readjustment|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 土地区画整理事業|1=land readjustment|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423116}} reduced the northern area and expanded the western part <ref name="sk3"/>.
The shrine was rebuilt on May 10, 1980 <ref name="sk4"/> and again on October 11, 2017.
== Rituals == [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinen-sai]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June, December), [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October), and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] (November) are conducted by a {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594490}} at the shrine. {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} (January 3), {{ill|National Foundation Day|ja|建国記念祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Foundation Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (February 11), {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天皇誕生日|1=The Emperor's Birthday|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q72318}} (February 23), and {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja|風日祈祭|1=Kazahi-no-Misai|2=ja|3=風日祈祭|WD=Q135524493|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524493}} (May, August) are held remotely <ref>Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 332</ref>.

Newly added western areas have [[:en:Viburnum odoratissimum|coral trees]] <ref name="sk3"/>.
== Access == ; Public Transportation * [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]]: About 300m southeast of [[:en:Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|Yamada-Kamiguchi Station]] <ref name="sk9"/>. * [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Yamada Line]]: Around 5 minutes' walk (450m) from [[:en:Miyamachi Station|Miyamachi Station]]. * [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] [[:en:Transit bus|Bus]]: 3-minute walk (300m) from "Miyamachi" [[:en:Bus stop|bus stop]].
; By Car * [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]]: About 8 minutes (3.35 km) north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Mikimoto Road). == References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史』伊勢市役所、昭和43rd year 3月31日、954p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 3月5日、1690p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[:en:Kogakkan University|Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:清野井庭神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17213398]] Wikidata name: Kiyonoiba Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17213398]] Wikidata name: Kiyonoiba Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyonoiba Shrine|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Kiyonoiba-jinja 02.jpg | location = 1-8 Tokiwachō, [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34.495|N|136.696722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Kaya-no-hime|WD=Q8191878}} | type = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor Rank), {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Before [[Enryaku era|Enryaku]] 23 (804 CE) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[Kinensai|Kinen-sai]] (Prayer Festival), {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|lt=Tsukinami-sai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (Monthly Festival), among others | location = {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }}
'''{{ill|Kiyonoiba Shrine|ja|清野井庭神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyonoiba Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Kiyonoiba Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Outer Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks 9th among the auxiliary shrines of the Outer Shrine.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): Volume 1, p. 12.</ref>
== Overview == [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]] Tokiwa 1-chome, 1197 <ref name="sk9">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 329</ref> (in the residential address system: Tokiwa 1-chome, 8) is where the shrine is located. There is a theory that the former shrine site was at the {{ill|Ise Takayanagi Shopping Street|ja|伊勢高柳商店街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Takayanagi Shopping Street|1=Ise Takayanagi Shopping Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11379159}} in Miyamachi 1-chome, Ise City <ref name="sk9"/>. If this theory is correct, it would suggest a different position for {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine|ja|草奈伎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine|1=Kusanagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228323}} and {{ill|Oma Kokuso Shrine|ja|大間国生神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oma Kokuso Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 草奈伎神社|1=Kusanagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228323}} as described in "Ruiju Jingihongen" <ref name="sk0">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 330</ref>. The "Ujiyamada City History" suggests that the original Oma Kokuso Shrine had a larger precinct and that Kiyonoiba Shrine was established on the current site based on the situation during its reconstruction <ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 936-937</ref>. The shrine grounds are rectangular, with an area of 1,095 square meters <ref name="sk3">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 333</ref>.
The {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deity|ja|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミ}} is [[:en:Kaya-no-hime|Kayanohime]] <ref name="uy"/> (Kayanohime no Mikoto <ref name="ic2">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 52</ref>). The deity is associated with the {{ill|irrigation water|ja|用水路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/irrigation water|1=irrigation canal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2935978}} near the shrine, though another theory suggests it is a [[:en:Bunrei|spirit]] of the deity of shipbuilding <ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 52-53</ref>. "Jinmyocho Kosho Saiko" states that the enshrined deity is the same as {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|ja|志等美神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitomi Shrine|qq=}}.
The [[:en:Torii|torii]] faces east, and the {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is located immediately to the right after entering <ref name="sk4">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 334</ref>. Built in [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei-zukuri]] style with wooden planks, it is surrounded by a [[:en:Tamagaki|Tamagaki]] <ref name="sk4"/>. There is no offering box.
== History == [[File:Kiyonoibajinja sengu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Scene from the reconstruction on October 11, 2017.]]
The name "Kiyonoiba" refers to the {{ill|wilderness|ja|原野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wilderness|1=wilderness|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410074}} around the shrine <ref name="ic2"/>, while "Iba" means [[:en:Weir|weir]] <ref name="sk9"/>. Over different {{ill|eras|ja|時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/eras|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=historical period|WD=Q11514315}} and {{ill|texts|ja|書物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/texts|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 本|1=book|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q571}}, "Iba" has been read as "iba," "iniwa," or "ite" <ref name="sk9"/>.
The shrine was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, meaning it predates 927. It also appears in the "Toyukemiya Gishikicho" <ref name="sk1">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 331</ref>, indicating its existence before 804. Rituals ceased by the late [[:en:Middle Ages|medieval period]], and the site was forgotten until {{ill|Oomiya-ji|ja|大宮司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oomiya-ji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮司|1=Gūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453500}} {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|1=Kiyonaga Kawabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92924151}} restored it on June 21, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: July 25, 1663) <ref>Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 329-332</ref>.
During [[:en:World War II|World War II]], the shrine was damaged in the {{ill|Ujiyamada Air Raid|ja|宇治山田空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Air Raid|qq=}} on July 29, 1945 <ref>Ise City (1968): p. 445</ref>. Postwar {{ill|land readjustment|ja|区画整理|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/land readjustment|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 土地区画整理事業|1=land readjustment|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423116}} reduced the northern area and expanded the western part <ref name="sk3"/>.
The shrine was rebuilt on May 10, 1980 <ref name="sk4"/> and again on October 11, 2017.
== Rituals == [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinen-sai]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June, December), [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October), and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] (November) are conducted by a {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594490}} at the shrine. {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} (January 3), {{ill|National Foundation Day|ja|建国記念祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Foundation Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (February 11), {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天皇誕生日|1=The Emperor's Birthday|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q72318}} (February 23), and {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja|風日祈祭|1=Kazahi-no-Misai|2=ja|3=風日祈祭|WD=Q135524493|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaze-no-hi-ki-sai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524493}} (May, August) are held remotely <ref>Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 332</ref>.
== Flora == The precinct has tall trees such as [[:en:Celtis sinensis|enoki]] and [[:en:Camphora officinarum|camphor trees]], along with mid-sized trees like [[:en:Quercus glauca|arakashi]] and [[:en:Zelkova serrata|zelkova]] <ref name="sk3"/>. Newly added western areas have [[:en:Viburnum odoratissimum|coral trees]] <ref name="sk3"/>.
== Access == ; Public Transportation * [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]]: About 300m southeast of [[:en:Yamada-Kamiguchi Station|Yamada-Kamiguchi Station]] <ref name="sk9"/>. * [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Yamada Line]]: Around 5 minutes' walk (450m) from [[:en:Miyamachi Station|Miyamachi Station]]. * [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] [[:en:Transit bus|Bus]]: 3-minute walk (300m) from "Miyamachi" [[:en:Bus stop|bus stop]].
; By Car * [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]]: About 8 minutes (3.35 km) north from {{ill|Ise Nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Mikimoto Road). == References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史』伊勢市役所、昭和43rd year 3月31日、954p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 3月5日、1690p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[:en:Kogakkan University|Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m06/0601.html 清野井庭神社（きよのいばじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}}
